There is an indoor gym next to the pool area. The hotel is in a good location for parks - wet n wild is 5 mins walk away. Universal Studios is just a mile down the road. We didn't drive, but a free shuttle bus brings you to all the parks. There is a nice TGI Fridays and a Chilis about 5/6 mins walk away. There is a small area in the lobby where you can get breakfast in morning (they serve starbucks coffee) and they serve tapas/snacks in evening. We didn' t eat there as we self-catered for breakfast, but the food looked nice. I would definitely recommend this hotel to someone travelling with children who wants a bit more space than a hotel and some of the comforts of home. It is in a quiet area, away from the madness of I-Drive. I found the staff at the hotel to be very friendly and helpful, we were always greeted with a smile. I would definitely stay here again.

reviews.author: Joseph Sipes
reviews.content: excellent attentive staff. the new pool bar was a great addition. The attention given by angie at the pool bar was great. and jose had good stories.

reviews.author: Abe Bernier
reviews.content: Husband and I stayed at THE POINT ORLANDO RESORT for one of our many stops for our honeymoon... Neither of us has kids, so while we understood that we were in the heart of Orlando's theme park district, we didn't want to be bothered with them the entire time. So choosing the Point and staying there was definitely a good idea.... everything wasn't perfect but we definitely would return.... The way that the rooms are set up is really nice... makes you feel that you are in an apartment.. Very spacious rooms for the price. The lobby is also very pretty.... The parking lot was never too busy, maybe because we were there during the week, but it was spring break... I did NOT like the stain that was on the floor in front of our room and every now and then it smelled like bad water in the hallways.. At one point I asked hubby did he let one go and he said NO... lol But overall, it was a nice experience and really really close to everything near Universal Studios, Wet n' Wild, carnivals, Downtown Disney, etc.

reviews.author: Adolf Krajcik I
reviews.content: We loved our stay here but, felt the pool was a bit small and quiet for 2 young kids. The pull out was uncomfortable for adults but, fine for kids. The room was super clean and we really loved having a kitchen and washer/dryer. The location was great as it was quiet but, close to International Drive. I liked it here and would stay again although I felt is was not a true 'resort'. However, the staff was great! OUr friends had a great room overlooking the pool (we did not) and their room was bigger (we had a 2 bedroom lock off)- it did make a big difference having the bigger room.

reviews.author: Kylie Abbott
reviews.content: Stayed for two nights at the Point Hotel (my second stay there). Everything was wonderful. The 1 bedroom suite is huge- with a full kitchen, washer/dryer, bathtub with jets and a pullout couch. We were close enough to walk to Universal Studios and City Walk and took a short cab back. The location couldn't be better- and the staff was extremely accommodating. The only place I want to stay when I go to Orlando.

reviews.author: Ewell Moore
reviews.content: My husband & I made the decision to stay here based on reviews we read on Trip Advisor but have to say that our experience at this hotel was one of the worst we have had. We have never had a problem with any of the hotels we have stayed at in Orlando before. This one disappointed us on every single level - only thing in its favour is the location as it is very convenient for Universal Studios and the International Drive area. However, there are much better hotels to choose from in this area and for much better, lower rates as well!! We figured we would pay more as we wanted to treat ourselves before we went on our cruise but it was such a bad mistake. The hotel was not very clean - in fact the carpets were positively filthy!! Also the bed was the most uncomfortable bed we have ever slept on and the result was that neither one of us got much sleep due to the sagging mattress and poor quality of the bed. They really need to invest in some decent beds in this establishment!!! After all, when you book to stay at a hotel it is in the hope of being able to get some rest before continuing with your onward travel - we were both exhausted after our stay at this hotel and found the staff to be very unhelpful and disinterested when we told them of the problems. There are much much better hotels to choose from in Orlando than this one and the biggest mystery to me is how this hotel is so high in the ratings on Trip Advisor as our experience there bears no relation whatsoever to how some of the reviewers have described it. It is as though they are describing a completely different hotel to the place we stayed at!!! I have to agree with the people who have left poor reviews and the best advice I can give to anyone looking for a place to stay whilst in Orlando is to do yourself a favour and give this one a miss.

reviews.author: Ervin Strosin
reviews.content: I would have never stayed here had I not read prior reviews and noticed that it was recognized as a Univeral Studios partner hotel. We were within 5 min of Universal and within only minutes of anything to eat. Wet and Wild Park is across the street. The hotel was clean, staff helpful, and was very nice!

title: Nottingham

description: small ensuite dorm rooms and private guest rooms, sea-front location.
name: Corran House

reviews.author: Lemuel Paucek
reviews.content: First time staying at Henderson's, and I was not disappointed. The front desk were very pleasant and accomodating, providing me with useful info for my stay, making sure I knew about the free continental breakfast and complimentary bottle of wine. The room could not have been any nicer, the bed was comfortable, it was immaculate with all I needed for my stay. I would stay there again and highly recommend it to anyone wanting to stay in Baltimore.

reviews.author: Aracely O'Hara
reviews.content: My wife and I stayed at Henderson's Wharf Inn for 2 nights. What an amazing place to stay. The hotel is incredibly charming and the service was amazing. A good continental breakfast every day and a bottle of wine at check in. The location is very good as well as many restaurants and bars/pubs are in walking distance in Fells Point. The only thing I would change is the bath tub, which was more like the type of thing you would see at a Holiday Inn than this type of place. Otherwise, the room and hotel was amazing.

reviews.author: Hailee Will III
reviews.content: I'm somewhat a very picky traveler when it comes to hotels. Based on reviews on Trip Advisor, decided to stay for 2 nights June 25-26, 2010 with Loyola University bound daughter. Based on availability we were going to have to change rooms for the 2nd night, however, the hotel accommodated us prior to our arrival to a harbor view room. This was the most amazing boutique hotel which I've stayed in. The beds were extremely comfortable, the view incredible and the morning breakfast excellent. Will definitely stay here again over the next four years. Thank you.

reviews.author: Aron Schmitt
reviews.content: This was our 3rd stay at Henderson Wharf Inn. This time we had a corner room facing the wharf. We will keep coming back. Comfortable, classy rooms, a free bottle of wine, and the best complimentary breakfast you will find. You can't beat the location if you want to visit the pubs and resturants in Fell's Point. Parking in free at the marina right across the street.

reviews.author: Eloisa Crist
reviews.content: My husband and I celebrated my birthday at our favorite place -- Henderson's Wharf Inn! We love Baltimore and all there is to do there, but more importantly than the town is the place -- and this is it! There is just this feeling of "ahhh" as you walk in, feeling like you've just entered the home of a dear friend. From the welcoming lobby (and front desk staff) to the wine in your room to the beautiful furnishings, you'll know within ten minutes of your arrival that you picked the right place! The rooms are very large and furnished like a bedroom out of a model home. You will not feel in the least like you are staying in a hotel! The mattresses and sheets are top of the line -- you'll get a wonderful night's sleep! The breakfast is just incredible with every sort of fresh fruit, crunchy granola, a large assortment of fresh breads and pastries, cheese and crackers, many fresh fruits -- you name it, it's there! I took pictures but somehow they got deleted from my camera card and I'm very disappointed because I've described this place to my friends, but it's not until they saw the pictures on my camera that they realized how truly unique and wonderful this place is. The front desk staff will attend to your every need and are so gracious and pleasant to deal with. The Inn at Henderson's Wharf combines the best of a Bed and Breakfast (charm, good food, warm ambiance) with the privacy and luxury of an upscale hotel. The water taxi comes within a few minutes of the front door -- it gets you wherever you want to go in Baltimore within minutes at a very low cost. We would never consider staying anywhere else when we're in Baltimore and in fact consider the Inn a destination in itself!

title: Oban

description: Corran Esplanade, Oban, PA34 5AE, +44 1631 562051. Rooms from £47 ppn
name: Oban Bay Hotel
title: Oban

description: As it is on the North Pier, rooms on three sides have sea views, some rooms at the back can be noisy from the pubs below.
name: Columba Hotel

reviews.author: Suzanne White
reviews.content: This inn was wonderful, beautiful and spacious rooms and bathroom. Our room had a great view of the harbor, I really cannot say enough good things. The breakfast had a large selection of fresh fruit, bagels and cereal with coffee, tea and hot chocolate available 24 hours a day. Ok the negative was, in Fells Point there are really only pubs and ice cream shops you must walk (20--25 minutes) or water taxi to the inner harbor for restaurants. But then you can return to the Inn and escape.

reviews.author: Zita Ward
reviews.content: We have four children and stayed in two rooms next to one another during the gap between Christmas and New Years Day. Extremely happy with the rooms; very comfortable beds, towels and very much liked the breakfast buffet.

reviews.author: Charity Goyette
reviews.content: I recently extended a business trip a few extra days, decided to avoid the chain hotels, and made reservations at Henderson’s Wharf Inn. It’s the smartest thing I’ve done in a long time! The inn is ideally situated in Fell’s Point, within walking distance to Baltimore’s Inner Harbor. The inn’s entrance, lobby, and rooms are nicely decorated and very comfortable. The inn provides luxurious robes, a complimentary bottle of wine, and a well-stocked snack and beverage bar. The Inn also provides complimentary breakfast, which is enough to get you going in the mornings. You can enjoy your breakfast, weather permitting, in their courtyard garden. It’s worth noting that the inn’s guest rooms are on the first floor with either marina or garden views. The floors above are residences, which add to the appeal. You never quite feel like a tourist, and instead feel quite at home. The cobblestone streets, shops, pubs (lots of them), cafés and restaurants of Fell’s Point might keep you busy and away from the Inner Harbor. However, if you do remember to visit the harbor, you're within walking distance. The walk will take you along the water’s edge, or you can just as easily take the water-taxis that will take you back and forth all day long for one fare. Please try the taxis at nights; the city lights are wonderful at night! I’ll be returning again, and will only consider staying at Henderson’s Wharf Inn!

title: Oban

address: Bella Vista, Tremeirchion Road, Bodfari LL16 4EG
description: 2 dbl (one ''en suite''), 1 ''en suite'' twin.
name: Valeside B&B

reviews.author: Miss Betsy Lowe
reviews.content: My mother and I stayed here for a bridal shower that was in the North end...so nice! Beautiful location, very close to restaurants/North end. Staff could not be more helpful and pleasant. I was sad to leave...would definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Damaris Graham
reviews.content: The convenience of this hotel can't be beat! It is the first stop on the Old Trolley Tour (a great way to see the city if you're a first time visitor), has a metro stop at the front entrance, is conveniently located across the street from a large variety of quick food stops and restaurants, AND offers scenic views of the Wharf. It is located next door to the aquarium. The water taxi stop that goes to and from the airport is located adjacent to the side entrance of the hotel. The water taxi offers a unique and fun method of transportation that is uniquely Boston! Also, the taxi is highly reasonable and efficient. There is a Starbucks on the ground floor serving light breakfast fare and most Starbucks beverages- a great quick breakfast stop for us! The location, friendly staff, and knowledgeable hotel concierge truly make this hotel an excellent location for anyone visiting Boston, but especially those that have never been before. Our room (located down the hall, away from the lobby, and with a water/Wharf view) was clean and comfortable; however, it was not quiet. The noise from the lobby echoes, (including lobby music and typical lobby hustle/bustle sounds), can be heard while in the room. The walls are extremely thin, so much so that we could clearly make out conversations from our neighbors. The Wharf, although scenic, is also quite noisy. Every morning, throughout the day, and sometimes late at night, the loud horns from the boats coming and going are easily heard. If you're up and out early, and out late, sightseeing and enjoying all Boston has to offer, this noise might not be a problem. After all, we didn't come to Boston to sleep and lounge in the room. The pro's definitely outweigh the con's here; Overall, a great place to stay in Boston!

reviews.author: Ms. Earnestine Nader
reviews.content: We were a family of six ages 5 thru 70 staying one night and we all had a wonderful experience here. Easy walk to Faneuil Hall, the North End and the Aquarium. Short taxi ride to the theater district or Newbury street. Excellent staff and service. The children had lots of fun using the pool after dinner when there's not a lot for young ones to do in winter. Rooms were very clean and comfortable. Breakfast buffet was plentiful and very good. We'd stay again.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: Campground with a local pub nearby.
name: Station House Caravan Park

reviews.author: Caesar Rempel
reviews.content: We have stayed here several times, at least once on the concierge level. This time we just had a standard room, typical Marriott, comfy beds, nicely furnished, granite countertops, single serving coffee/tea maker, mini frig, about average size for a hotel in the heart of the city. The location cannot be beat - the T is right outside, as is the harbor, the aquarium, the (very good) Legal Seafood restaurant and, of course, Quincy Market and the North End. Even the tour buses and harbor tour boats are right there. Also positive - great view of harbor from our room, a new lobby with a very welcoming bar/restaurant, with tv screens everywhere including some for your private viewing in the booths. A few negatives - no newspaper delivered to the door, USA Today and WSJ are available in lobby but no local newspapers. Would be nice to have the Boston Globe to check out local events and get a feel for the city. For the first time, our stay here was very noisy. We are silver Rewards members with standing request for quiet room, which has always been honored in the past. Our room was on the fifth floor, but it fronted on the atrium, right over the bar/lounge/restaurant. Not sure if they had a convention meeting there or just a large crowd, but it was noisy well into the night. I would definitely ask for a room away from the atrium if we return, and stay elsewhere if they couldn't guarentee it. Also, they charge for internet use, even in the lobby and the business center. They do have computers in the business center for free use, but center is not very private (no door, and it's open to the lobby). I also found that I could not navigate on their computers as I would on my laptop, there seemed to be some limitations -- and I was not trying to access any racy sites!! Just gmail, trip advisor, and some event listings. The towels were also extremely thin, not at all up to the standard that the price of the room would suggest. The room lacked a magnifying mirror, which is surprising with all the business people (incl women) who stay there. Altho the new lobby was nice, we did miss the seating near the Starbucks downstairs (the ground floor has just Starbucks, registration & lobby are up a flight). Before the renovations there was seating for Starbucks so you could have breakfast there or relax with coffee and pastry in afternoon. Now it is "to go" only, so you'd have to bring your coffee and treats back to the room or eat/drink on the go. Wondering if they eliminated seating to encourage guests to have breakfast in their newly renovated restaurant??

reviews.author: Travon Little
reviews.content: I have been to this location before children a number of times. However, did not realize how unsafe the inner balcony design was until my 22 months old son tried to climb. We almost lost him, I just turned around for 2 seconds to open the hotel door when I saw him half way up on the balcony. There is no safety measure built under, so it's a straight drop to a few floor down. It was a scary experience. Tried to write and talk to someone in the hotel regarding this matter but no one seemed to notice or care. Also, there were a lot of drunken people being very loud outside the corridor in the middle of the night.

reviews.author: Louvenia Harber
reviews.content: We had a wonderful time staying at this Marriott. We stay in Boston quite often, but usually dont stay right down on the water. We had the kids and thought this would be fun. This hotel is wonderful, right across the street from teh aquariam, shopping, and food vendors. Parking was also very, very easy as they will valet everything. It was surprisingly easy to get to, but we knew how to avoid the traffic. We did see some of the notes regarding the construction, but to be honest, we never heard a thing. Looking forward to going back

reviews.author: Reginald Goodwin Jr.
reviews.content: We took the kids to Boston for a sightseeing weekend to kick off summer break. I debated whether to reserve two rooms or pile into one room. We have one pre-teen and two teenagers. I ended up reserving one room and requesting a roll away. We arrived very late on our arrival day (nearly midnight) and were relieved the roll away was already in our room. The hotel was under renovation, so felt compartmentalized and rather plain, but the cleaning staff added her own sense of sparkle to our stay which dramatically improved our opinion of the hotel. (Smiles, fresh towels, spotless room, etc...) The location was picturesque and easy. You can hop on the T literally right outside the door and there were dining options within steps of the hotel. One evening, our tired kids grabbed dinner across the street at Quincy Market and headed back to the hotel while my husband and I had a leisurely dinner at Legal Seafoods, also across the street. We did not spend a lot of time at the hotel, but found it convenient and comfortable for our stay. Positives: location, comfortable beds, excellent staff. Negatives: a little noisy.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: Has 40 pitches and 4 caravan sites.
name: Llyn Rhys Campsite

reviews.author: Mrs. Dovie Harber
reviews.content: Seeking a convenient location to Quincy Market and activities, we stayed here on our last night of vacation. The hotel is under construction but they had someone there to guide you where to go at all times. We were able to check in very early. Unfortunately the room we were given was right above the outdoor bar area which was very loud on a Saturday night. We called the front desk around midnight to see how late the bar would be hopping and when we got our bill in the morning, they had comped us half the cost! We were very impressed with the management. The rooms are very comfortable and clean. But the location is the best thing about this hotel (besides the Starbucks in the lobby).

reviews.author: Olga Wunsch
reviews.content: When travelling we usually stay in one of the Marriott family hotels with good experience. However, we found the experience at Marriott Long Wharf below Marriott standards. At time of booking (early June) it mentioned construction was to be finished by mid May 2011. Big surprise to find out at checking in that construction was far from over. First morning 7:10 AM we woke up at the sound of heavy construction machinery... Unacceptable! We were moved to another section of the hotel which was far less noisy, but ..... why put guests in rooms so close to the construction in the first place when other rooms are available??? Besides the construction sounds, the hotel is built in such a manner that the noise from public area below, lobby and/or events areas cannot be blocked from most rooms. That means when in your room you will certainly hear kids running around and doors being shut(throughout the night!). We were 'forced' to enjoy the music of a wedding taking place in the events area and sounds of breakfast taking place in that same area since the restaurant was temporarily under construction. Finally, service is courteous but certainly not friendly or warm. Rooms are spacious, neat and housekeeping is good. Marriott: Yes! Boston Marriott Longwharf: No!!

reviews.author: Keshawn Lockman
reviews.content: My family and I travelled to Boston for spring break, this hotel was a great choice. Step outside and the Boston waterfront, Fanueil Hall and much more is right there. They are doing construction on the lobby areas which did not affect us at all and will be done soon. Lovely pool and hot tub, great views from our room and super nice staff. The price was more than I'd hoped to pay for this trip but all in all, it was worth it and the kids LOVED it (ages 8 and 12).

reviews.author: Anastacio Kilback
reviews.content: We have stayed in or near Boston a few times before, but this was our first time at the Long Wharf. We were able to get a decent rate as a Marriott Rewards members - $195/night (although still a little hard to swallow with tax and daily parking fees but it was our five year anniversary). The location was by far one of the better places we have ever stayed in Boston. It was not even a five minute walk to Fanueil Hall/Quincy Market, and the North End was less than 10 minutes away. It was right along the water, which we probably would have taken advantage of more had the weather been nicer, but it was still a beautiful area with plenty to do. We also took advantage of one of the trolley tours which in some ways was like door to door service because the main stop is on the side of the hotel. I would definately return to the Marriott Long Wharf, and think it would be a fabulous place for the kids. Besides the aquarium, and other area attractions, there is a small park next to the hotel that has a small playscape and there is even a carousel just outside the back entrance. I cannot really comment on the service or amenities because we did not use any of the facilities or have a need to call the front desk or house keeping. The only somewhat of a downside is that the hotel does not have an area for free coffee and fruit (not a necessity, just a nice perk) although there was a Starbucks on the lower floor and there is the option to make it in your room. We were not expecting a free breakfast with our stay, so to keep the cost minimal (we splurged on lunch and dinner) we just grabbed bagels at Quincy Market in the morning. Overall, it was a very nice stay, and based on the location alone, I would definately recommend it to anyone with or without children. Our stay was pleasant, our room was clean, the beds were comfortable, there was a big screen TV in the room. In and out, our needs were met. Wish the price could have been slightly lower, but we knew that going into our stay that we could get a place with a lower price and free breakfast just outside of the mainstay. . . but then you lose the ambiance of staying in the city.

reviews.author: Ms. German Schowalter
reviews.content: We went to Boston to tour the city and stayed here. Overall, this is a nice hotel with a great location. Very close to Quincy Market, NE Aquarium, the waterfront and the Italian section of Boston. Rooms were clean and the pool and gym are excellent. Only complaints are relatively minor...we expect that for the price you would get queen beds instead of twins. Also, service by the bartenders in the restaurant and one of the bellhops did not meet my expectations for a hotel of this calibre. Otherwise, would definitely stay there again due to location and cleanliness of the hotel.

reviews.author: Cheyanne Rohan
reviews.content: We chose this hotel because it was close to the attractions we wanted to visit: aquarium, IMAX theater, Quincy Market, etc. We were steps away from the wharf where the cruises launched and the trolley bus tour also departed from the corner in front of the hotel. We stayed for two nights and the room was OK - we had requested two double beds, but were given a King room with a cot. The space was pretty tight with the cot in the room and the bathroom was on the small side. The room was very clean and the beds (and cot) were very comfortable.

reviews.author: Celestino O'Conner
reviews.content: I have stayed at the Marriott Long Wharf several times while in Boston for both business and pleasure. Overall, it is standard Marriott fare in the lobby, lounge, and restaurant. Everything is adequate and the beds are great. However, the hotel does also offer a great view from most rooms and an excellent location for visiting the historic and business areas of downtown Boston. Overall, I recommend this hotel and have chosen it myself for most visits to this fine city.

reviews.author: Lukas Murray
reviews.content: The Marriot is located right next to the Aquarium, the wharf with all of the different ferries to various places, Fanuel Hall, and the gem of the city, the North End. You can just park your car and walk any where you want, or hop on a shuttle and tour the city. It was nice and clean, quiet even though it is a huge hotel, and very comfortable, even with 4 adults in one room. The bathroom doors were a little odd - it was two small doors that opened out into the room. So they were sort of in the way at times, and not that easy to shut in a hurry. But the view was great and the stay was pleasant. I am looking forward to going back, even though it is pretty pricey to stay ($300 to start), the location is well worth it.

reviews.author: Lisette Hodkiewicz
reviews.content: We were attracted by a recent special which was run by Marriott. We stayed two nights in a room which actually had a view of Long Wharf and the boat docks. It was a quiet, spacious, well-ventilated, comfortable room, clean, with some nice new touches since our previous stay at least four years ago. Thereâ€™s a nice flat screen TV, and a refrigerator to replace the mini-bar (nice touch). Pillows are a little too soft for our tastes, and I doubled up on mine. The breakfast buffet is as well-equipped as it ever was. As we experienced in the past, check-in was quick and professional and the staff remains friendly.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: [[Bed and Breakfast]] with 3 bedrooms (1 ''en suite'') welcomes families and also has camping facilities. Evening meals and packed lunch (£4) available on request.
name: Glan-yr-Afon B&B
price: £30 pppn (£25 for >2 nights) includes guests' sitting room and own entrance

reviews.author: Tiffany Kiehn
reviews.content: I would never ever stay at this hotel again...Inside the hotel is run down and the lobby is nothing spectacular.. I know it is an old hotel but they really could modernize it.. I stayed here with my boyfriend for a concert.. (and believe me we are not picky.. we are only in our early 20's) and hated it.. we got a suite and there was only the bed, desk, and two chairs in the room.. it was just tons of empty space with no seating area.. and a really tiny bathroom.. our smoke detector was hanging out of the ceiling and all of the paint in the room was chipped.. The location is fantastic and the places to eat connected to the hotel are very good.. but the location itself does not make up for how awful the hotel is.. I thought for a city like Boston it would have some charm.. but nope.. stick to the Back Bay Hotel or the TAJ

reviews.author: Franco Mitchell
reviews.content: We were terribly disappointed by this hotel. During our first night we were awakened by screaming and pounding on the door of the room beside us by some man who was clearly intoxicated. This lasted for quite a while and was actually quite scary. However since it was NYE we hoped it wouldn't occur the next night. The next morning the room service dishes that were sitting outside that room since early the day before had still not been cleared. Far worse was what we saw running through our closet and then under our door into the hallway.....a mouse!! I was horrified. After calling down and explaining the girl said "gross" and promptly transferred me to someone who offered a room upgrade and to comp that night for us. The next room had a mouse trap under the bed (empty thankfully). Obviously this is an ongoing problem for the hotel which they have failed to adequately address over the years.

reviews.author: Julius Fahey
reviews.content: We stayed here for one night on Jan 2, 2012. I booked the hotel after much debate after reading mixed reviews on TripAdvisor, Google, and other travel sites. However, for a price of $102 for a Deluxe King Room, I couldn't argue. Our room was on a corner of the building and was sizeable. The linens were fresh, the bed seemed fairly new, the carpet was plush and everything was really clean. They even had a sizable LCD TV, with actual HDTV. Most places you go have the LCD TVs, but regular standard def. Anyway, the room was perfect for what we needed, a place to relax in the evening and get a nice night of sleep. I didn't encounter any issues with the thermostat, I simply lowered the temperature to 68 and it was perfect. I could even cut off the heating/cooling unit, had we gotten too hot or cold, so those complaints from other reviewers are unfounded. Complaints about the bathroom are valid, it was pretty small. Barely enough room for the shower, toilet and sink. However, being a guy I don't have an issue with it, as I shower, or use the sink and toilet and get out, and don't plan on spending hours at a time in front of a mirror. For that, it was adequate. Also you should remember that this hotel was built in the 1920s, so its historic and things may be small. In the end, this hotel is a great spot to spend a few nights. Its got a great location, its relatively cheap, and its easily a 4 star hotel. Is it 5 star? Definitely not on account of the bathroom. I feel like some people may be a bit picky on this site and expect a Ritz Carlton. The fact is, the price is much lower than many other hotels in the area, so any 'issues' you may run across are compensated for. Also, the complaints about $12.50/day for wifi are valid, BUT you have to remember EVERY other hotel in Boston, as well as most chains - Marriott, Hilton, Ritz Carlton, charge that amount or more for wi-fi. Its a way for hotels to make money. Back in the 80s-90s they made it off of phone calls, but now with cell phones they have to make up for it somewhere. If you don't like it, go to a Starbucks, McDonalds or get a smart phone like the rest of the country.

reviews.author: Kiel Mertz
reviews.content: There is much to be said about this hotel that is great. The location is excellent - the lobby is old world style elegant and well-maintained. The front desk staff is very helpful and courteous -might be the fastest check-in service ever. We booked a standard king room, which turned out to be two small rooms that had been combined. Good size space in the room, and there were two bathrooms. Here's the caution - they were the tiniest bathrooms I've ever seen. On one side were the toilet and bathtub and no space in between. The sink was at the end of the bathtub. There is minimal counter space and most of it was taken up by the rack holding shampoo, soap, etc, plus a tissue box and cups. So just beware -- not all the bathrooms in this hotel are the same. Bed was very comfortable and there was a flat-screen tv in the room. The view was non-existent - both windows looked out on an air shaft and another building. There was no closet - only a free-standing wardrobe about 20" wide. (The bottom of this was very dusty, as I found out when a pair of dark slacks fell off a hanger.) One inexcusable oversight - I got out the extra blanket that had been neatly stored in a zippered bag in the wardrobe - hence the fallen pants - and when I opened it, discovered that it had multiple holes cut out of it. It looked like someone had tried to remove burn spots but only made it worse. I dropped it on the floor and it was left there the entire time of our visit. Kind of made me wonder if the room ever got vacuumed. I would stay here again because the price was right and location very convenient.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: 6 ''en suite'' B&B bedrooms with TV, DVD, radio alarm clocks, hairdryers, bath robes. Also self-catering accommodation. Camp site has ceased.
name: Moors Farm
title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: 1870's Gothic mansion set in 280 acres of mature gardens and tranquil parkland with mullioned windows, oak panelling and beautiful staircase. 10 individually designed rooms and suites, all ''en suite'' with unlimited Wi-Fi. No pets allowed. Afternoon tea and home made cakes in the ''Offas Dyke Bar''. Lunch 12.00–14:00, Dinner 18.30–21:00 served in one of 4 restaurants: the ''Cwm Dining Room'', the ''Mahogany Panelled Restaurant'', the ''William Morris Room'', or the ''Shropshire Lounge''. Local ingredients and suppliers are used wherever possible with many ingredients traceable to source. Graded *** with Welsh Tourist Board. The most luxorius camp site I have met. Camp by lake next to facilities block. Phone owner for entrance code. Pay £5 pppn (2014) in the morning to groundsman. 5 star hotel next door are polite but claim to know nothing about camping as its under separate ownership. Hotel
name: Mellington Hall Hotel

reviews.author: Stephen Graham
reviews.content: End of a wonderful trip saw us land here. In a nutshell - rude bell staff (note I will not tip poor service and bad attitudes just because you expect it), rude front desk staff and staff who had to be kept out of the room - you may have been desperate to clean it but check out is 12 (and no later). This hotel has the smallest hotel room I have ever seen in the USA. Poor beyond words. Great location but that's all it's got going for it (don't be hopeful from the grand entrance and internet pictures!)

reviews.author: Madelyn Rolfson
reviews.content: I spent one night here on a quick getaway to do my own historical walking tour. The hotel touts its own history and though nice, I was not particularly impressed overall. The Park Plaza seemed almost as though it was trying to be too many things to too many people...more than one wedding booked (large lighted ,glossy photos and names of couples in lobby detracting from the historic decor), lots of high maintenance vacationers, European travelers, even airline flight crews. Amid the shuffle, I felt a bit overlooked as a lone traveler. At one point, I waited in a long line for the Concierge to ask for a walkable map of the historic African-American sites trail, only to be told hurriedly that they didn't have any but "the trail starts on the other side of the park". The group in front of me in line however, booked a boat tour, made dinner reservations for a family of 4, and got directions from the Concierge. All I wanted was a map. Maybe it would have been better had I not been traveling in the middle of the summer? In any case, the rooms have a nice historic flair (skeleton key doors) and are small but perfectly acceptable. The room was clean but my bed sheet was stained, which was pretty unexpected considering the caliber of the hotel. Overall, I could not have asked for a better location and for the seasoned traveler who needs little direction or attention, it was a reasonable choice.

reviews.author: Fabian Langosh
reviews.content: I booked a junior suite at the Park Plaza for our family vacation. Upon check in, there were no junior suites, so we were upgraded to some fabulous 2-room suite on the 15th floor of the Towers. It was lovely and the 15th floor suites include complimentary evening appetizers and morning breakfast. The 2-room suite accomodated us nicely and we were very gracious for the upgrade. However, the airconditioning would not go below 77-degrees (It was set to be 64-degrees when we arrived)!. Granted, on day one, Boston got hit by the near 100-degree heat wave. On day 2, i made 3 requests between 9 am and 10:45 pm for the AC to be looked at and i also requested a portable fan for the room. Sleeping night number one was miserable, difficult and hot! Anyhow, after my 3rd request for assistance, and it was now 11:15 PM and I needed to go to bed, so i walked down to the front desk and nicely explained my unhappiness and discomfort. The desk clerk appologized profusely and gave me her portable box fan from behind the counter. I was happy to have a fan at last, but displeased that my 3 earlier requests were disregarded. On day number 3 the room temp with AC would still only go down to 77, although the weather out was pleasant and mildat 78-degrees. We opened our windows and slept with them open because it was cooler out than in. All other aspects of the hotel were grand. The location right by a T station and 1 block from the Boston Public Garden can't be beat. The hotel is not cheap to stay at, and we were still paying a good chunk of change to stay there regardless of recieving an upgrade, so it is not accecptable to disregard a guests request for room comfort. Still, despite the AC situation, I would recommend this hotel and I would stay there again.

reviews.author: Makenzie Towne
reviews.content: We stayed on the 15th floor in the Towers. The price was very reasonable for a major city hotel ($130/night) and if you get a room on the 15th floor, you have access to a private elevator, free wifi, a private lounge with free continental breakfast and afternoon hors-d'oeuvres. The room was very clean and comfortable. The location is fantastic - a block away from the Gardens/Boston Common, two blocks from the shops in Back Bay, close to the beginning of the Freedom Trail. We caught a Red Sox game and actually were able to walk all the way to Fenway Park without much problem (took us about 40 minutes). You can also walk to the South End where there are some great restaurants.

reviews.author: Trevor Predovic
reviews.content: I found my room to be somewhat dark and gloomy but I suppose that relates in part to the age and historic ambiance of the hotel.The garage parking was expensive,$44 per day.Also they charge for internet access and local calls.The staff however were helpful,especiaaly the concierge Frank Gallagher.He recommended Old Town Trolley Tours who were excellent.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: accommodation and food
name: The Oxford Arms

reviews.author: Ashtyn Hansen
reviews.content: The Park Plaza is a beautiful, historic hotel in a great location near Boston Common and all of the shopping and restaurants in the Back Bay area. They have retained the historic quality while updating the hotel so the common areas (lobby, restaurants, and meeting space) are really nice. For a business traveler with all expenses covered, it is a great hotel. For anyone else, it is rather expensive even beyond the room rate and Boston taxes that apply to all the hotels in this area. The Park Plaza basically has no parking so you must use valet parking that was $44 a night (plus tips), which seemed very high even by Boston standards. Web access was over $10 a day, and it was unreliable. The bars and restaurants were expensive compared to other places in the neighborhood so leave the hotel to eat. I would generally not recommend it for families as the historic hotel rooms and especially the bathrooms are too small for more than one or two people. There is also no pool.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: toilets, showers, washer, dryer, fridge, freezer, children's play area.
name: Radnor's End Apartment & Campsite

reviews.author: Ryann Ratke DVM
reviews.content: My wife,son and myself were supposed to stay here from saturday until monday this memorial day weekend. We stayed on the 7th floor--We could only stay one night--The room had a urine smell,stains on the carpeting and the couch--The toilet is so close between the wall and tub I actually had to put one leg in the tub to use the toilet.' The A.C doesnt work,the lobby was very humid and the elevators to slow for the amount of people--Im sure during its time it was a fabulous hotel but not now--Im sorry

reviews.author: Karson Farrell
reviews.content: This hotel is a classic full city block hotel with a beautiful lobby to relax and meet people. It is very well maintained with a well trained staff. The hotel is located in a part of the city with plenty to do... a few minutes from shopping on newbuyr St. , the Public Gardens/Common and the theater district. Many excellent restaurants within two blocks of the hotel. Great experience. Rooms were clean and comfortable.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: Wi-Fi.
directions: in a cul-de-sac off Lion St
name: Davlyn B&B

reviews.author: Dannie Hintz
reviews.content: The rooms were clean. The linens were nice. There was no wifi in the rooms-strange in 2011. The entire experience was outdated. But the service was excellent.

reviews.author: Marianna Williamson
reviews.content: We paid extra to be on the "Concierge Level". These "recently renovated" rooms are no different than the rooms on the lower levels. No view and the same small "charming rooms" as in the rest of the hotel. The lounge amenities aren't enough to account for the difference in cost. The street noise (despite a room on the 15th floor) kept us up all night and didn't quiet down even on Sunday morning. The hotel is in a nice spot with great access to many downtown offerings but there are far better options in the area.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: Accommodation and food service; Free Wi-Fi.
name: The Old Pandy Inn

reviews.author: Edna Considine Sr.
reviews.content: Location excellent, atff worked really hard especially conference food/beverage/IT, meertings,. Rooms small and dated (bathroom- think cruise ship) but typical for a city location. What really ruined my weekened was the 3 calls a day I needed to make to security to report my neighbors on the 7th floor who were smoking non stop. Colleagues reported the same on the 8th and 14th floors. Our clothes, towels and linens really smelled bad. The hotel is non smoking and has a fine policy but clearly this not enforced, Worse is that in such an old building, a fire would be a disaster. The hotel needs to be proactive and police their smoking guests!!!!

reviews.author: Eldora Bayer
reviews.content: This was the second we stayed at this hotel in the past five years. The location is great with the green line a block away and the commuter rail about four. The lobby is beautiful and has the golden era ritzy feel. The rooms are small and need a little freshness (the hotel opened in 1927). Service and staff are helpfull, polite and professional. Parking, room service and the restaurants are pricey; there are better deals elsewhere (MJ O'Connors). We stayed on the coldest days since 2006 and the hotel had some heating issues (along with the rest of the city). They did what was humanly possible to help and it really wasn't an inconvience. Would I stay here again? Yes for the right price. If you go in the warmer times of year you can walk to almost everything. And if you can't walk the green and red lines are nearby.

reviews.author: Fernando Lebsack
reviews.content: Staying at this hotel was fantastic and a highlight of my honeymoon to the USA everything was top shelf and reasonably priced. We stayed in the Towers suites and would recommend to anyone. Cant wait to stay here again when visiting Boston.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: Accommodation and food services.
name: The Park Hotel

reviews.author: Lacy Bergstrom V
reviews.content: I really dont think you can get a better value for your money than staying here. Great rate, very classy, great location, terrific value. By the way, some of the most comfortable pillows and cases ive had at any hotel. Will stay again and would recommend.

reviews.author: Neva Gerhold
reviews.content: The hotel was everything we had expected from the Park Plaza, until there was a knock on the door. We certainly had not expected the front desk to send us up a bottle of wine and a plate of cheese and crackers with a personalized note extending their wishes for us to enjoy our fundraiser that evening. Remarkable customer service in an already wonderful location and beautiful hotel.

reviews.author: Jan Goyette III
reviews.content: This was our first stay at Park Plaza. It would be hard to have a better location: a half block from the public garden and all the shopping of Boylston and Newbury streets. Just a short walk to Trinity Church (a must see), Copley Place, Prudential Center. The hotel is quite lovely - spacious lobby, lounge, and numerous restaurants. We splurged for a concierge level room (they call it the tower level) at a conference rate of $365 per night, including tax, so quite expensive I think. Our room was spotlessly clean, the king bed was very comfortable, and the large flat screen TV worked ok. The bathroom was miniscule. In fact we could not open the door all the way, because the toilet blocked the door! And the bathtub has this very strange gel-like padding on the bottom. It made for a rather dangerous shower, since you had to worry about keeping your balance. Just don't close your eyes, or you might fall over! The size of the room and bathroom are typical of older, historic hotels. But this bathroom was REALLY teeny. The concierge level had a nice lounge that served an ok breakfast and a nice afternoon snack with a cash bar. Overall the Park Plaza is an ok place to stay. Just don't expect a large room like you might get at the newer hotels. It's an historic building. Great staff, very accomodating.

reviews.author: Desiree Luettgen
reviews.content: Excellent location and conveniently near transport options. Very comfortable rooms and courteous staff. A very historic hotel with nostalgic ambiance. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Shania Franecki
reviews.content: I cannot say enough good things about this hotel. It is very elegant. Everyone there is accommodating. They are just simply amazing!

reviews.author: Mozelle Kub
reviews.content: Located very close to train station. Frank was ablle to get us very good tickets to a Sox game at the last moment. Really liked the "little things" like offering us an umbrella to use when it was pouring rain outside. We have stayed here several times and the service has always been super!!

reviews.author: Clair Labadie III
reviews.content: Having read the reviews for the Boston Park Plaza I was a little worried, but my worries were put to rest after I discovered the exact location and walked into my nice air-conditioned chilled room!! We went July 16th weekend when highs were in the mid 90's! I made it a point to request a room with a king and air conditioning that cranked when we checked in and the front desk came through!! This is a historic hotel with lots of character! There are a ton of dining opportunities in the building itself or right across the street, Legal Seafood, McCormick and Schmicks, PF Changs, among others. Our room was huge and had two separate bathrooms as well (free upgrade to Deluxe)...the bathrooms were on the small side but nice to have my own bathroom!! The shower pressure was great and there was plenty of hot water! The bed was super comfortable with both down pillows and regular foam pillows. TV wasn't plasma or LCD but was 32 inch and had plenty of channel opportunity. coffee maker was a krups machine with 4 servings to brew! The fitness Center wasn't your typical 10x10 room....they had several precor machines, free weights, a yoga room and strength training machines! If I go back to Boston I will definitely stay at the Boston Park Plaza! We booked through Priceline and got a fantastic deal!!!

reviews.author: Earnestine Kautzer
reviews.content: I stayed twice at the Boston plazas and had slightly different experiences. It's located in Back Bay, which is a great area that is accessible to many things and nice to walk around during the day. At night, it's quite deserted and there isn't even many take away or fast food options if you are looking to eat (I had to eat food from a CVS store..). The check in the first time was breezy, however he wouldn't let me use my credit card that still had the generic card as I hadn't received the new one. The signature matched my identifitication.. but anyway, go to the atm he tells me. The atm was out of service, so i had to walk to a bank. Minor inconvenience, but inconvenience in any event. The lobby is very nice and there are many staff members on hand to assist you in you need help. The room was very-very large (as big as a suite almost) and had 2 double beds. There was a desk area, lcd and windows that opened to a sheltered roof that clearly many people sat out and smoked on. The bathroom was large in size with a full bath and seat in there, so perhaps it was a disabled access room (explains the size?). My only complaint about the room was the noise associated with the old heat/cool system, so loud. The room was next to the ice machine too, so some noise. The breakfast was sufficient (fruit plate, berries, muffins and coffee with juice) and there were no complaints with service otherwise. There was a candy/drink machine in the iceroom which was convenient also. But they didn't accept many coins or notes (evens ones listed, just shoots them back.. randomly), so I think they need updating or calibrating. The second stay I was placed in a much smaller and dated room. The bathroom was a lot smaller, but still had a bathtub. The tv was older however, but there was an nice street view available. I received an express checkout slip under the door the night of leaving, which was missed during the first stay. The girl checking me in accepted my credit card with no questions. All in all a pleasent stay, some inconsistencies within the hotel - but with a hotel of this size.. you half expect it. Gym is small but open 24 hours. Some of the equipment needs updating.

title: Offa's Dyke Path

description: Bed and breakfast overlooking the North Norfolk coast. Set in 3 acres of park and woodland looking over the coast from the village of Sedgeford.  Villages such as  Burnham Market, Brancaster, Thornham and Holme are a few minutes drive away.
directions: Sedgeford is signposted at Lavender Corner on the main road into Hunstanton
name: Magazine Wood B&B

reviews.author: Mrs. Layne Gulgowski
reviews.content: There were many benefits to this hotel- suites (good for putting small children to bed before parents), shuttle (with VERY courteous, thoughtful drivers- though you have to schedule everything in advance), GREAT housekeeping staff (the best I've ever had in a hotel), nice view of the river (and the traffic next to it). The downsides were- The pool was COLD, and the hottub was cool. The restaurants were VERY expensive ($20/person for the breakfast buffet- we ended up buying peanutbutter and yogurt for our room instead), the parking was $35/day, Internet was $10/day (we didn't indulge), the bathroom didn't have a fan and the closest T stop was a 15 minute walk, over some rough sidewalk terrain (hard with the stroller) and if there was anything going on downstairs in the conference area you could hear it in your livingroom, along with all the doors on the floor slamming shut. The concierge was always very helpful. The frontdesk amended the bill quickly when we were overcharged compared to the rate we booked over the phone. The shuttle was a nice way to get into town, though we found trying to schedule our returns too difficult and just took the subway and walked. Overall, we had a good time and it served its purpose of a good home-base for a long weekend away.

reviews.author: Zola Bradtke
reviews.content: -- Basic hotel -- feels like it is used a lot -- feels a bit like an airport hotel - Restaurant staff were EXTREMELY friendly - Perhaps a bit too busy at times-- two glasses stayed outside my door for two days -- they were there when I checked in! To their credit, when I let them know -- they were gone in 20 minutes.

reviews.author: Melisa Swaniawski
reviews.content: As Marketing Manager for a major corporation, I cover the New England territory and stay frequently in local Boston area Hilton hotels. I stayed at the Doubletree Boston in early April 2010 for business reasons - this was my first stay at this particular location. From the moment I entered the lobby and went to the front desk, I experienced the BEST (and I mean BEST) customer service that I have experienced in a long time. The front desk personnel were helpful and friendly, and went out of their way to make me feel welcome. Knowing that my family and I were planning to come back for a few days in late April to college hunt as we live in a nearby state, I cancelled reservations at a competitors hotel, and booked again at this location for a stay from April 22nd - 24th. Once again, the customer service was AMAZING!. Now, for the important part....the room was clean (not exceptional - could have been a bit better), however, it was cozy, the view was incredible, the beds were super comfortable, and everyone who helped us was AWESOME. DJ at the front desk - *****, Frederick the Concierge, *****, Delmy, Housekeeping,*****, Ty - Engineering/Maintenance, *****. This hotel is doing something very right and I applaud the General Manager of this property for giving some leeway to the staff to offer the little extras that make the guest feel important and welcome. In a day and age where we have too many choices, I can assure you, this location will be my first choice for both business and personal travel to Boston. Lastly, the breakfast buffet was excellent, and the shuttle to Boston was super convenient. I hope that the staff at this hotel continues to treat guests in the same manner, as this is noticed, and so important more than ever today!

reviews.author: Brant Waters
reviews.content: and Warm Cookies too. really nice breakfast buffet - can get a package to include it - made to order omelettes and pancakes. Dinner was awful - raw prime rib, cold soup - don;t eat in restaurant but breakfast very nice with great views. Ask for room with river view. Can get free parking in a package - ask for it. Front desk vewry nice. TVS in living room and bedroom didn;t work. Heat in bedroom didn;t work. But free coffee in room and Starbucks for charge in lobby. Very nice bed - comfy. Really in Cambridge - not really in Boston. Not a good location for Freedom Trail - have to take a car or bus to tourist things.

reviews.author: Reece Ferry IV
reviews.content: The room was spacious enough and had a great view of the Charles River. The service was excellent. The concierge made great recommendations for nearby dining. The only negative things I have to say is that the location is a bit odd for sightseeing although the shuttle is convenient enough or you can walk to the T (try to avoid driving into downtown Boston if you can unless you want to pay an arm and a leg for parking). The other thing is the value for the room itself is good but then I had the feeling of being "nickel and dimed" to death. I had to pay extra for the parking, I had to pay for internet service, breakfast was not included (most of the suites hotel I stay at have a breakfast bar). That sort of thing. But all in all a very pleasant stay and I would recommend it.

reviews.author: Nyasia Adams
reviews.content: I have stayed at the Doubletree Suites Boston fairly often. In my most recent review I alluded to the encroaching ravages of age in the hotel. (In me too, but that is another matter!) The hotel has been refurbished and refreshed: microwaves have been added and furnishings updated. The location is still a matter of taste. It is 0.9 miles to the nearest T stop at Central Square, but I do not mind the walk. They still charge for on-site parking, which seems incredibly petty. They still charge for private internet, but the free public internet does reach virtually all of the rooms, and is adequate as long as you are not downloading files, etc. Once you cut through some of the penurious policies of the hotel, it is clean and spacious with a pleasant staff and comfortable suites that are often quite affordable by Boston standards. It is helpful if you like to walk!

reviews.author: Holly Metz
reviews.content: My husband and I drove from Vermont to see a Red Sox game, and spent one night at the Doubletree Guest Suites. We booked the room on Priceline and were pleased at getting such a good rate. When we arrived at the hotel and went up to our room, we found a beautiful 2-room suite with TWIN BEDS in the bedroom. Priceline had given us no indication of this, of course. Well, this wasn't what we had in mind. I called the front desk, and a very pleasant young man listened to my story. He could not have been more helpful. "I've seen that with Priceline reservations, people don't know what type of room they're bidding on. You'll want a different room, of course... let me see what I can find for you... I have a room with a king bed on the 6th floor. Would that be all right?" Goodness, yes, I replied; what will the extra charge be? "Oh, no extra charge, ma'am, we're happy to accommodate you." Rarely do I see this level of service when I travel. What a pleasure! The hotel itself is beautiful, with a center atrium, 14 floors, glass elevators... beautiful views from the room of rowers sculling on the Charles River in the morning. The hotel's shuttle brought us to within a 15-minute walk of Fenway Park, which was very helpful. The suite itself was roomy, spotless, with plenty of room to sleep, work, relax, snack. Big screen TV in living area, second TV in bedroom. Mini fridge, microwave and coffee maker were very welcome. My ONLY quibble with this hotel, and many others -- in this day and internet age, it's silly to charge ($10/day?) for high-speed internet. It would be like charging for telephone or TV 30 years ago. Lesser hotels all over the country offer internet/WiFi for free; it's time for the Doubletree (and the Boston Four Seasons, for heaven's sake) to do the same. In any case, this hotel would make a wonderful base from which to explore Boston.

reviews.author: Ben Jacobs
reviews.content: My husband and I went to Boston to see a jazz show at Sculler's Jazz Club. The jazz club is located in this hotel so we decided to stay there. We arrived around 1pm and our room was not ready but they held our luggage and we had lunch in the restaurant. The suite was on the 8th floor and was very well laid out and we enjoyed it. There is a separate bedroom and living/dining room and they are separated by the bathroom and fridge/microwave area. All the suites look out on to the center atrium area and it is a bit noisy but not enough to disturb your sleep. The jazz shows were excellent and we bought the dinner and show package for one of the shows and it was a good value. It was a 3 course meal with an excellent choice for dinner and then great seats for the show.

reviews.author: Mae Ritchie
reviews.content: It is hard to find good service these days, but the staff at the Doubletree was fabulous. I stayed there with my family, including two children, for four nights and was pleasantly surprised by their warmth and hospitality. The suite is perfect for families. And the cookies were delicious!

title: Old Hunstanton and Hunstanton

description: A relatively large mid-range hotel very close to the Eiffel Tower, in a nice, quiet and green area of Grenelle. Despite the closeness, only about a quarter of the rooms, designated as such and commanding higher rates, feature a view of the tower itself. All of the rooms and common spaces have been renovated and feature modern decor inspired by the tower and the metro. The rooms are very functional and feature operable windows, but the amounts of plastic and the violet theme may not provide for a romantic Parisian experience.
name: Mercure Paris Centre Tour Eiffel

reviews.author: Javonte Friesen
reviews.content: We have stayed here many times. I am a third generation guest. My husband and I always come to Daytona for our anniversary every year and stay at tropical manor. This is one of the cleanest places we have ever stayed at. They have everything you need in a room plus more. this place is small but it's perfect if you want to stay somewhere quiet. This trip, we were threatened with possibly not making it to Daytona due to an illness. Last Minute, we decided to go. Although I was terribly ill for the first 5 days of our trip and didn't get to enjoy one minute out in the sun, we still made it out every evening to see the ocean, walk in the sand, and pick up shells. On our last day, Audrey, the owner was gracious enough to let us stay the entire day in our room and on the sundeck until the evening hours. there were hardly any guests left so we had the place practically to ourselves. Audrey even came down and joined us by the pool! We appreciated this time, since I was unable to thoroughly enjoy myself at first. I felt great the last day and my husband and I left feeling very happy. This is what true hospitality is all about! Thank you again Audrey and tropical manor. We will be back again next year!

reviews.author: Tony Nitzsche
reviews.content: My sister and I stayed here, Perfect getaway spot for families or a place to relax. Clean, no bugs, great staff, great view, heated pool. Quiet. Cozy. Bring coffee. Enjoyed the visit VERY MUCH! A short few feet to the beach. Barbeque grill. No bugs. Kiddie pool. Would definately visit again!!! DO NOT HESITATE TO STAY HERE! Cozy rooms..great maid service. On top of it groundskeeper. Friendly staff. Perfect place to relax. NO LOUD guests. Very homey and relaxing experiance. Stay there, you won't be disapointed!!!!!! A*****!!!!!!!!! Will visit again!!!!!!!!!

reviews.author: Cheyanne Nader
reviews.content: My family and I have been going to Daytona for about 20 years now. For the past 15 years Tropical Manor has been our Tropical Paradise one week out of the year. The people who run Tropical Manor are extremely polite, willing to help if there is a need. The rooms are kept cleaner than most peoples homes. Just be careful if you have trouble climbing stairs because some of the rooms don't have elevators that go to them and the steps are a little steep. Over all we have always been pleased with the Tropical Manor and will stay there for years to come.

reviews.author: Roger Auer
reviews.content: I recently stayed in this hotel and found the experience very pleasant. Paying more than reasonable price, you will find rooms clean and comfortable, and staff friendly and helpful. Not to mention that hotel is located close to the shopping area. Next time I will visit Montreal, I am definitely coming back.

reviews.author: Lorena Collier DVM
reviews.content: What a pleasure to arrive for breakfast each day to a smiling face that was truly happy to see you and help. Lassandra kept the line moving and made sure each person was treated special. She is truly an asset to your organization!

reviews.author: Randi Mills
reviews.content: I recently stayed at this hotel for business and was very impressed with the customer service that I received. The front desk staff were very friendly and courteous. The room I stayed in was spacious, clean, quiet and comfortable. The location was perfect and very close to my office. The best part of my stay were the delicious pancakes and egg white omelets whipped up by Lassandra. A great way to start your day!

title: Paris/15th arrondissement

description: Not to be confused with the other Mercure, this one is not far away but in quite a different setting, facing the busy Boulevard Grenelle and the overground Metro 6 line. Neither as quiet nor as modern or comfortable as the other Mercure. On balance it is one of the cheaper hotels in its class close to the core tourist centre of Paris.
name: Mercure Paris Tour Eiffel Grenelle

reviews.author: Timmy Metz
reviews.content: I was traveling on business to Indianapolis this month (Oct 2011), and was unable to locate a good hotel rate downtown (the Colts were playing that weekend), so stayed on the north end at the Embassy Suites. The lobby was very nice, with efficient check-in, clean room and bathroom and a simple, but good, breakfast. Maybe because it was a Sunday night and/or low occupany rate, that the only "hot" breakfast items were omelets - no sausage, pancakes, etc. However, it was fine and was not a big deal. I like Embassy Suites and always enjoy my stay.

reviews.author: Turner Bode
reviews.content: Our son graduated from Indiana University, Bloomington in May of this year. I began my hotel search in January. I thought that was enough time....wrong! All hotels anywhere remotely close to the school was booked solid. If there were a few rooms left, the price to accommodate all my family members was almost enough to pay another semester at IU! To my dismay, I search and searched. Finally I found Embassy Suites-North in Indianapolis. It was a drive to the University, but I didn't mind because the price was somewhat reasonable and the staff was awesome! Lassandra, one of the cooks at the food court, was amazing! She always greeted you with a smile in the morning and her omelets were to die for! Also, Supervisor, Debbie Flemming-Little at the Front desk, was very nice. When we checked in she was the only person at the front desk, but she handled our family and the other guests checking in with skill and finness.

reviews.author: Miss Benedict Langosh
reviews.content: The hotel of choice for our Indy business travels. Staff treats guests like family, notably, our breakfast chef, Lassandra. She has a way of making every customer feel special and at the same time accurately and efficiently handle our breakfast orders.

title: Paris/15th arrondissement

description: Located in a quiet corner of Montparnasse, and if you don't mind a 10-min walk to the Metro, this is a nice and relatively new hotel. Easy access from the Montparnasse-Bienvenüe Metro: just take the stairs all the way up to the train platforms, exit on Boulevard de Vaugirard, take a left and follow the street up to the hotel. There is a Velib' City Bike Rental park just outside the hotel.
name: Novotel Paris Gare Montparnasse

reviews.author: Meaghan Effertz
reviews.content: We changed hotels during our stay in Indianapolis from a 4-star hotel to this Hampton Inn. This hotel beat out the 4-star hands down in every category possible - and was a lot better priced. This hotel is very conveniently located downtown - close to everything! We were here for an event at the convention center and it's a very easy walk down the street. The staff (thank you Megan!) was over-the-top helpful. The rooms were nice and clean. The bathroom was good-sized. There are mirrors in the room so that more than one person can get ready at a time in the morning (always a bonus in our family). And best of all, it was quiet! The beds are double, not queen - so on the smaller side. There is a table and two chairs instead of a desk, which is nice for eating takeout or playing games. But the outlets are next to the table for computer usage. There is no refrigerator or microwave in the rooms. They do have a good variety of tv stations and pay movies available. The breakfast is free and is what I would call a continental-plus. There are some hot dishes available as well as the basics - and it's managed by some very sweet ladies who are very attentive. Also, the breakfast room is on the 2nd floor in a very large room with lots of space to move around in. A nice change from the usual squeeze-and-eat of most hotels with free continental breakfasts.

reviews.author: Gudrun Franecki
reviews.content: We have stayed here for three Big Ten Basketball tournaments and have loved our stay. The location is ideal (only one block to Conseco Arena).The staff at the front desk and breakfast staff are terrific and do their best to accomadate your every wish. This year we were forced to check out early because our son had to have emergency heart surgery and the staff was very helpful and sympathetic.Also one of the young men at the door ran almost two blocks to flag down an airport bus. We were not able to tip him,but called a friend and she gave him a tip which he was reluctant to take-Said he was just doing his job. Looking forward to our trip and stay in 2012.We also love meeting and interacting with fans from other Big Ten Universities.cannot not mention the breakfast and staff who work there. They are so helpful and energetic and hard working. I could say more good things ,but better stop. You get the idea.

reviews.author: Jasper Walter
reviews.content: Just got back from your hotel for the Dealer Expo. This is the second year we've stayed at the Hampton Inn Indy Downtown. We love this hotel! The staff is wonderful, so very friendly; the front desk staff, valet and breakfast attendants are friendly and helpful. This year we were upgraded to a suite with a jacuzzi. That was a nice treat for us as we are hardly ever able to get away for a weekend. I can't wait for next year's Dealer Expo and our night at the Hampton Inn!

reviews.author: Hazel Schumm
reviews.content: I loved this hotel and it's staff! I will stay here again for my next Indy trip! I must say the breakfast area was better than expected and kept us from paying to eat breakfast out! When a staff member found out that I couldnt sleep without the noise from my fan ( like at home) she made sure a fan was placed in my room that day while I was away! (Thanks so much!) While downtown take advantage of the $5 cabs to any other downtown location! Great deal!

reviews.author: Pink Sipes
reviews.content: This is a great location for visiting Indy. The only problem we had was the panhandlers on the street as you walk. The hotel was clean and the staff very friendly. The valet parking guys were very fast if you needed your car. This is next to the circle center mall and lots of restuarants. Try the Weber Grill.

reviews.author: Kristofer Kling
reviews.content: Mine was an unplanned stay at the Hampton Inn & Suites. My flight had been canceled due to weather in Chicago and the alternate flight I was booked on had been oversold. For me this meant an extra two unscheduled nights in Las Vegas. The hotel provides 24 hour shuttle service to the airport and the driver did an excellent job of accomodating my need to go back to the airport several times over two days. The front desk staff was very kind in making sure I had everything I needed and even made recommendations for nearby places the shuttle could drop me for shopping. The breakfast staff and housekeeping were all very attentive and the room clean and comfortable. I can definitely recommend this hotel as a place to stay if you want to be near the airport off the strip.

reviews.author: Rico Keeling
reviews.content: This Hampton Inn is new and the perfect place to stay if you are going to the airport the next day. It is located one street away from the McCarran Airport. The service is excellent, and the rooms are outstanding! Everything is so simple and clean there. The breakfast was good with plenty of options.

title: Paris/15th arrondissement

description: In the heart of the Passy village and its fashion boutiques. It's the first independent hotel in Paris to have been awarded the European Ecolabel. Fully air-conditioned, free WiFi.
name: Hôtel Gavarni

reviews.author: Travon Will
reviews.content: I booked 3 nights at the El Cortez for two friends and I just to prove what a great hotel it really is, and they were knocked-out with the great room, service and food, just as I had anticipated. First, check- in was quick, and our Tower Strip-View room was very cool inside with outdoor temps hovering over 110... The classic hardwood furnishings and decor pleased my most particular friend. The beds were extremely comfortable. The bathroom was of adequate size, regardless what others have posted here...We're average sized guys and none of us felt confined in there. Plenty of hot water for our 3 long showers, and the drain drained properly too... The soaps, shampoos and etc were of fine quality, and supplied daily with the thick towels provided. What more could we ask for? The room was quite soundproof, and we didn't hear any noise from other guests at any time during our stay, and we slept at all sorts of hours too. The housekeepers did an excellent job of cleaning daily, so a hefty tip was in order! The casino is great for us low-rollers, and we were able to play for quite a long time on our budgets. Cocktail service was excellent too, no matter where in the casino we were. That $1 tip brings that server back often! Oh, the food was great, especially with our $5 'Fun Book' coupons. A large Prime Rib dinner with all the etc in Kitty's for $6 was primo! The breakfast buffet will have you full all day too. All the El Cortez servers were top-drawer and courteous... I now have a couple more friends to join me for a gamblin' vacation anytime for little $$$, and we highly recommend it too!

reviews.author: Linnie Predovic Sr.
reviews.content: Great place to stay. Very cordial staff. Let me check in very early without question. Tower rooms are the best deal. They are very large and comfortable. This isn't a ritzy strip type place so don't expect it to be. For a Downtown place it can't be beat. Probably not the best for first timers or youngsters but for the middle aged crowd and up it is ideal. Lots of security and very comfortable. Good food choices in all price ranges.

reviews.author: Willow Mosciski
reviews.content: We booked this hotel via travelworm.com for $60/nt for two nights on Dec 28-29. We got a two-queen-bed Tower Room above the garage. The room is big, the beds and sheets are clean, but the bathroom is small. It's a smoking room, but we felt comfortable and didn't sense any bad smell (we're not smoking persons). It's located in LV downtown, two blocks from Fremont Experience, and about 5-10 minute drive to the strip. They also have free parking lots, but no free internet. So far, everything is good as we spent most the times out of town and just needed a place to sleep. However, the lobby/casino area is very stinky with smoke. It's so stinky that we didn't even care to use their free $20 gambling coupon as we had to run into the lift every time we had to pass this area. Fortunately, we rent a car and could access the lift from parking lot. Overall, it's good value hotel for budget-minded travelers. If you need an up-scale hotel, it may not suit you.

reviews.author: Dr. Mabel Kautzer
reviews.content: Got two rooms for $49 each. It is smokey downstairs and on some of the lower floors you can smell it. It was a good way to ease into the Vegas style, where you could be overwhelmed on the strip. Cheap and open tables could always be found. People say that is in the dodgy part of downtown, but i dont think that at all. Never felt that way at any time walking around at night. They have a security guard right on the corner as well. Just one block from the Freemont Street Experience. If you want to save a few bucks while staying downtown I would recommend here.

reviews.author: Maxime Armstrong
reviews.content: The place seems to be remodeled. Tower rooms are spacious, well designed and clean. Staff was courteous. There is a lot of construction around the hotel but it was a minor inconvenience. For the price we found we could take a cab to the Stratosphere pool which is way nice. Close walk to Fremont Street. We never felt unsafe outside at any time of day or night. The Casino was not as upscale and peppy as Main Street Station or the Golden Nugget but again, for the price and close walk its a great place to sleep. This is an up and coming place with the urban renewal occuring in the area. The south side has a great view of the strip skyline. Take advantage of the the low price.

reviews.author: Anabel Wyman
reviews.content: Plaza vs El Cortez? After seeing the reviews and the recommendations.......we chose the El. 2/8-2/12 3pm check in time, but earlier check in was no problem. We had a great room (tower) with double queen beds on the 12th floor (Rm 1207) facing the strip. With our 2-4-1 coupon found in the American Casino Guide (a must to buy, but check the book when you receive it in case there are missing coupon pages if you buy thru Amazon or E-bay) and our meager slot play return, we payed $277.20 for 5 nights for a fantastic stay. Soda and ice machines on each floor. Don't put their Vegas tap water in a water bottle to save $ from buying high cost water on Fremont St or the strip. Tap water tastes horrid but suitable to brush your teeth with. Front desk clerks were sooo nice and very helpfull. They had lost our information on our stay, so thankfully I had brought a copy of our confirmation that was done thru their web site. I recommend that everyone print it out and bring it. We asked for a fridge, which was rolled in on a nice size cart that we were able to set up our own one cup coffee maker on top of. My husband loved that . Also the love seat with coffee table which he loved relaxing on after a long day of walking. The t.v was a bigger than what we saw in the other reviewers pictures and worked great with lots of channels. Again, nice to relax to for the short time spent in the room. Plenty of super hot water, great water pressure (even during peak usage times) and tub drained quickly. No water backed up. The bathroom and large window in the room opened up (4 inches) for ventilation. The beds were soooo comfy & very clean. The curtains darkened the room so we never knew what time it was in the a.m. Housekeeping never bothered us in the morning when we had the "do not disturb" sign on the door. Great!! We were there 5 nights (including weekend) and thought we had the whole floor to ourselves because we only ran across two people in the hall on our last night. Very quiet rooms.
