I chose the Springhill Suites since it was pretty new around the time we stayed last year. The rooms were nice, clean and very modern. It is off the strip across from the Las Vegas Hilton if any of you know where that is. We always rent a car so getting to the strip and around was okay for us. The free buffet breakfast was nice. Nothing elaborate but more than enough. Eggs, Bacon, Potatoes, Oatmeal, Cereal etc..... The price for the hotel was good and I would stay there again if I didn't want to stay in a Casino hotel or needed to save a few bucks but I usually stay at the huge hotels on the strip.

reviews.author: Isai Price
reviews.content: We stayed at SpringHill Suites when we were on our national Knocking Out Obesity tour. We traveled through 28 cities and stayed at many hotels. This was our favorite! Beautiful, great room design and full of all the amenities you need.

reviews.author: Mariano Brekke
reviews.content: I stayed four nights at this hotel with my husband. The room was very roomy, clean and comfortable. We could see the view of the nearby golf course and the bright lights of the Las Vegas strip from a distance from our room. We felt safe and liked the idea of having a security pass to even get to our floor; this prevents people from just walking around. The hotel is very quiet in the evenings. The pool on the roof top was a treat, especially the beautiful views of all Las Vegas. We also enjoyed the very delicious breakfast buffet each morning. The free parking in a well lighted and secure parking garage is also a plus. I will definately stay again at this location and recommend it to all.

title: Shrewsbury (England)

description: A reasonably priced B&B near Coed-y-Brenin with four ensuite rooms which can be either double or twin rooms. Beautiful Farm 2008, Regional Winner.
name: Cae Gwyn Farm

reviews.author: Leonard Altenwerth
reviews.content: This is a huge, beautiful resort but definitely not worth the money if you don't have time to take advantage of the amenities. For $343.00 per night (and that is a discounted rate) we had a rather small room with two queen size beds. The room smelled of cologne. Presumably from the previous guests. Hopefully not from the housekeeping staff. Our room was 62 degrees when we got there and we never could figure out how to raise the temperature. We had just come from the Renaissance Resort near Seaworld where we spent $111.00 a night for a room that was twice as large, with a sofa and chairs, dining area, wet bar and office area. Plus, everything in the Renassance is right there - restaurants, bars, pool. No long walks required.

reviews.author: Gladyce Wuckert
reviews.content: It was easy access to Magic Kingdon but longride back at night. The boat sevice was not good after the park closed and going to other hotels not great either. Lots of fun for Princess that we had with us but very expensive to do all the extras (like tea with sleeping beauty) Not worth the price $250.00 for 2, but you did get a doll. Concierge service was great. Housekeeping was great. Food is OK but room service was fast with warm milk in the mornings and at late night. Would probably not go back but try Hotel in Orlando Ritz or Marriott and rental car next time.

reviews.author: Ella Schmitt
reviews.content: This resort is beautiful but I didnâ€™t like it as much as the Animal Kingdom or Polynesian. This may be because I was traveling with a 5 and 1 year old. Enjoyed the character breakfast and the staff were pretty friendly. Had the meal plan so we didnâ€™t spend a lot on food. I've stayed at the several Disney properties and this would be the one I would least likely return to. But again, it may be because of the smaller kids. I do, however, love the spa there and each time I go made sure I get a treatment. They have a kid's pedicure that my 5 year old loves, she has had it twice and it put her to sleep each time.

reviews.author: Kiara Morissette
reviews.content: I had stayed at the Grand Floridian before, as at the Polynesian and Contemporary. This time I went with my family, including two children, for New YearÂ´s Eve; As everyone knows, the place is very handsome, but surprise! as soon as we got there we were warned that we could not get a meal inside the hotel unless previously reserved. eVEN BREAKFAST! With two children, we decided not to submit them to long lines and had room service every day. For other meals, we had to go to another hotel or one of the parks.That seems to me absolutely ridiculous. The hotel was clearly overbooked andnot worried over their guestsÂ´well-being. For the sort of price you pay there, I felt definitely cheated. They should not fill to overflowing, making it imposible for their own guests to have a meal inside the hotel. And more: I asked once to use an internet, which I imagined I would have to pay at some business center. Front desk told me where to go - it was closed and I was told to use the one at the Contemporary, where we had had dinner three nights. Well, I will stay elsewhere next time...

reviews.author: Brando Boehm
reviews.content: We just returned from our 1 week stay at the Grand Floridian and already miss it! We've stayed at other Disney properties before, and this one is by far the best yet. The rooms were large & comfortable for our family of 4. The in room fridge allowed us to have a quick in room breakfast every morning. The bathroom was large and the robes and coffee maker provided a sense of luxury. The heated pool was inviting, although we never had time to take a dip. There were plenty of people indulging, and even though the pool is open 24 hours and our room faced the pool, it was never loud. We were in the Boca Chica building, which was at the back of the property, overlooking the beach and the pool. Our daughter looked forward to the chocolates provided with the turn down service. The location was convenient. It was fastest to take the Monorail to the Magic Kingdom (no stops) and the boat back (no stops). The monorail from the MK back to the GF had 4 stops before the hotel. We ate at the cafe for breakfast twice, and it was tasty and had the wonderful friendly Disney Service.

reviews.author: Santa Rippin
reviews.content: We just returned from a three night Disney Wonder Cruise followed by three nights at the Grand Floridian. The hotel is very lovely and takes one back to the elegance of the turn of the century. Staff was exceptional to Disney standards. Multiple restaurants on the "pricey" side. This was a trip for our Grandson and although the hotel very nice, probably not overall the best selection of a Disney luxury property on the Monorail for a seven year old. The lobby is gorgeous complete with high tea and a full orchestra in the afternoon and evening. The grounds are gorgeous with multiple pools; one with a sandy beach. Great access to the Magic Kingdom; the first stop going on the Monorail and first stop returning on the boat. One evening we ate in the casual Grand Floridian Cafe where they have display drawings done by youngsters while waiting for dinner. My husband inquired if they would display our Grandson's for just a short time giving him the opportunity of being the "Artist of the Day". It was a highpoint in his trip. We were in the Conch Key Building on the fifth floor in a gabled room with balcony. The room was generous in size with a king bed and sofa making into a single bed, flat screen TV, clock radio/alarm with IPod feature and large desk and work table. The bath has first class amenity shampoos and lotions with double sinks and separated from the toilet and tub/shower combination. Each building also had a small laundry room with two washers and two dryers. I learned in Florida, since we shared our visit with Tropical Storm Fay, it is better to have a property mainly contained in one building rather than one with multiple buildings. One could get pretty drenched walking from the main building and the Monorail stop to the Conch Key building where our accommodations were located. Overall, we would return but probably not with a small child and would look at either the Polynesian or Animal Kingdom.

reviews.author: Christelle Douglas
reviews.content: We approached our booking at the Grand Floridian with a hint of trepidation. We had read mixed reviews about the hotel and were slightly nervous as Disney virgins. However the experience was wonderful. We were upgraded to a concierge room which was tremendous. The facilities were first class as were the staff. The location is tremendous for the Magic Kingdom and the bus service to the Animal Kingdom was swift and frequent. The top tip though is to take the boat to the Magic Kingdom. It is pleasantly cool in the humid conditions and not well used. On some journeys we were the only passengers. All in all, a great hotel.

reviews.author: Xzavier Funk
reviews.content: The hotel rooms are not thoroughly cleaned. The room fittings/furnishings are worn/show excessive age. The electronic door keys have known problems (extraordinarily inconveniencing after a day at park) - It defeats convenience of a quick return from the park to your room. No sound proofing between rooms. Only basic channel options for the television, the internet is slow, costs extra and isn't wireless. Food is standard Disney fare but at higher prices and even expensive meals have no table cloth. Finally, it seems to get higher ratings than deserved. The Gaylord Palm near Celebration could never have these issues and get its high rating. I think there were a lot of times when the desk/GF management/WDW management just didn't feel it was necessary to fix blatant problems. I strongly suggest visitors snap pictures of exactly what you find wrong and uncorrected and post them here upon return home. Unfortunately I didn't think to do that at the time, I kept thinking each thing would be the last and I should just focus on enjoying the trip as a whole. Finally, read through the recent 3 and 4 star review texts, the content is more accurate of the hotel and I don't see how people gave it 3 or 4 except maybe some categories brought the average higher than reality.

title: Snowdonia National Park

description: An Edwardian Country House available to hire for self catering holidays. Sleeps 14 - 18. Visit Wales 4* Accommodation. The guest bedrooms consist of four double ensuites, two family rooms and a twin room. Sea and mountain views.
name: Plas Gwynfryn

reviews.author: Zoey Dooley
reviews.content: Maybe it was because we had high expectations (for over $600 a night!), or maybe it was because we had heard such positive reviews, but we went to the Grand Floridian with a very optimistic attitude. From the minute we arrived things started to go wrong. Our first room was not a Magic Kingdom view, even though we had paid extra for it. The next room was barely a view, but we did not want to move again. The rooms are very modest, and the balcony was tiny. Then after all this fuss we were all thirsty, but the bar key was missing. We had to make several calls to the desk to finally get someone to come after close to an hour with a key so that we could spend some morre money on water! The room was very average...and not so clean. We found a pacifier near the bed, and gum on the bathroom floor - neither was ours (YUCK)!!! The front desk/concierge were never helpful or well-informed. We requested room service menus (again, to spend more money!) and they were never delivered. Once when we were coming home from the parks at 11pm the door to our part of the hotel was stuck. Our family, and several others, were stuck outside in the torrential rain until someone came to open it. We waited almost 30 minutes, after a long day in the parks, which we all felt was unacceptable. Breakfast was mediocre, and we never could get refills on our coffee! The shuttles took forever to come. The pools were O.K., albeit very crowded. Nothing spectacular. My husband and I have traveled extensively...both with and without children. Neither of us is particularly picky or high-maintenence Yet we both agreed that this was our worst hotel experience ever. This is unfortunate, as the Grand Floridian is very expensive! I wish I could go back in time and book a more moderate resort. Then even if there were some snafus we could more easily overlook them. I have never written a bad review/complained about a hotel before. Please head my advice and stay somewhere else!!!

reviews.author: Ms. Cyril Ward
reviews.content: We checked in on December 14, and checked out on December 17. My birthday was the 15th, and this was my birthday present from my husband. The hotel was beautiful. We stayed in Building 9, which was Big Pine Key. Our room was very nice, it had 2 queen beds. When my husband checked in they asked if we were celebrating something and he told them that it was my birthday. So on the 15th, when we came back from the parks, there was a plate with candies and cookies and a 10" tall white chocolate Mickey MOuse, along with a personalized card from Minnie. I felt that this was quite nice. We ordered room service, it was very nice, although it was over $50 for a salad, a fruit plate, a cheeseburger and 3 canned cokes. The monorail was very convenient, although it was crowded at times. The beds were wonderful. However, the walls were quite thin, I was not feeling well, so we stayed in the room all day, and we could hear people talking in the hallway. We had a lagoon view, we could see the Polynesian, but there was a tree directly in front of our balcony, if it had been summer, I doubt we could have seen anything, but all the leaves had fallen off the tree so we could see the hotel and the water parade, however if was hard to get pictures as the branches were in the way. Housekeeping was wonderful, as was the turndown service at night. I would have expected free internet service, but there was a fee. Overall I really enjoyed the experience, but I do not think it was worth 435 dollars a night.

reviews.author: Carolina Shanahan DVM
reviews.content: We stayed here Sept 27 - Oct 7 and were very suprised. Having stayed at the other deluxe properties and always thought that this property would be too frilly and too victorian. To our suprise not at all. As you can see we found ourselves here by the Contemporary overbooking. But being Disney they had everything done and even better to make sure that your "magic" was not ruined. Not only were we upgraded to the Grand, but were given concierge service and a Magic Kingdom view. Service was exceptional all requests granted and everyone went above and beyond. Common areas vey nice, room always spotless, dinning very good and just an overall wonderful experience. If you have a daughter,a great place for your little "princess" to stay and feel like royalty. Keep in mind it is still disney and not a four seasons. Lastly, can not say one bad thing!

reviews.author: Gunner Hodkiewicz
reviews.content: My girlfriend and I spent 4 nights at Disney's Grand Floridian. The hotel was very clean and just about what you'd expect for a hotel in this class. Room service was on top of their game, and would often show up on multiple occasions during the day. The only complaint we had was more on the fault of Disney's Magical Express service (gets your bags from the airport & to your room when you arrive), but other than that, very nice stay. They have a 24 hour "restaurant" located on the premises where you can get drinks, pizza, sandwiches, etc. any time of the day. This was helpful on more than one occasion. It also had at least 2 different pools with nice lounge areas. Location is about as good as it gets, having the monorail right outside your room! A bit pricey, but location is everything.

reviews.author: Sadie Keebler
reviews.content: We stayed at the Grand Floridian for 9 nights from 21st June with our 2 year old son. The Hotel is fabulous with an impressive entrance and excellent staff. We arrived at the Hotel, tired and jetlagged but our spirits were soon lifted by the excellent staff and service and the first view of our room which although didn't have one of the more expensive 'Magic Kingdom' views overlooked the pool and was lovely. The staff were continually friendly and the maids did an excellent job of cleaning the room with a turn-down service in the evenings. In the mornings you can also see the maids doing a little parade through the gardens before they start work! The Hotel is across the lake from the Magic Kingdom and provides an excellent view of the Wishes fireworks from the jetty, or beach (or if you are lucky enough to have a view from your balcony!). There is also the monorail which is one stop away from the Magic Kingdom and is excellent with a little one. We dined at the cafe and at the grill with the food being very good and a variety for all. I would recommend this hotel to anyone and can't wait to visit it again one day.

reviews.author: Monte Mohr
reviews.content: Having stayed @ most of the Disney Deluxe Resorts, I was a little apprehensive about spending the extra $$ to stay here. turns out, totally worth it! We were checked in right away and upgraded to a better view. Upon learning that it was our anniversary, champagne and flowers were sent to our room. The service was outstanding everywhere. We had drinks @ both Narcoosee's and Citrico's as well as dinner @ V & A. All together, awesome! The 2 minute monorail ride from the MK was great and I loved laying by the pool and hearing faint MK sounds (the train, the parade etc) The beach is really great too, lots of cozy cabana's! It was easy to get a drink @ the pool bar too! Did I mention the beautiful live orchestra in the lobby each evening? Wonderful! Or how my husband forgot a shirt and had to buy one for dinner in the store and they quickly ironed it for him to wear that night? All in all, an amazing experience!!!!

title: Snowdonia National Park

description: A family-run inn. The owners live on-site and see to the upkeep of the hotel's amazing gardens. Breakfast is served in the main building.
name: Solvang Gardens Lodge
title: Solvang

description: A locally owned and operated Best Western. A few blocks from Sonoma Plaza, this hotel is pet friendly and has a pool, jacuzzi rooms and free breakfast. Free wifi and laundry facilities you can use. Expensive for a Best Western - but not your typical, either.
name: Best Western Sonoma Valley Inn

reviews.author: Sam Schulist
reviews.content: Well. the rooms where ok, but you could hear your next door neighbor visit the bathroom. The Staff was ok. Not too friendly but just ok. the GOOD things about this place is, that DADELAND MALL is near. A nice MALL for great shopping!

reviews.author: Libbie Osinski I
reviews.content: The hotel was very family friendly. A good breakfast was served daily; the staff was very friendly, professional and helpful. Staff responded to issues promptly. The location was close to main attractions and the airport. I would recommend this hotel to anyone. There was also a gated entrance to the parking lot.

title: Sonoma

description: A comfortable inn with a Santa Fe feel. Further from the Plaza, a cab, car or bike would be ideal. The more affordable mid-range option compared to it's neighbouring Best Western. They offer a pool and hot tub, 24-front desk services, and a complimentary continental breakfast. Know someone who lives in Sonoma? Have them make the reservation for you - they offer a locals discount!
name: El Pueblo Inn

reviews.author: Danial Spencer
reviews.content: We booked two suites at this property for a week. The suites are big enough, but lighting is poor, furniture uncomfortable. Nice TV's though. My grandson enjoyed the pool--no one was ever there. The staff was very friendly and responsive to any request. There was a managers reception on only one night of our stay--not every night like our past experiences with Residence Inns. The parking is a nighmare---not enough conveniently located, and the gate often does not open. There is a call button, which will get it to open--but it is a hassle. Our bill was charged for multiple daily parking fees, although we only had one car. Check your billing carefully---overall I expected much more for the price--we could have had individual rooms at another property for less. The location is convenient. This property needs some updating---the price is based on location only--not what you get in ammenities. The breakfast seems to rotate every two days---eggs in a bag and lots of carbs! Long waits for the waffle maker. The strength of this property is the staff, which exceeded expectations. We would probably not stay here again should we return to the area however, as it is not a good value relative to the cost.

reviews.author: Delpha Cole
reviews.content: Good service, the room is simple but well decorated, the bed, sheets, pillows, were comfortable. The employees are kind, overalls in the restaurant during the breakfast, always willing to help. The location of the hotel is excellent!! either for business or vacations like it was my case, there are restaurants and stores very close, a lot nightlife action!!. The breakfast is very complete and comfortable area. My only complaint is about the elevator that is extremely slow, we had to wait up to 10 minutes to be able to ascend, since we were in the floor 5. I would recommend it to any person.

reviews.author: Aniyah Ryan
reviews.content: Good deal for the price Our stay at the Golden Nugget was very pleasant. Our room was clean and quiet and the staff friendly and more than eager to address our needs. The manager gladly offered us another room because the first room we had needed a lot of work even though we were only there for three nights. He explained that nothing had been done to the place because they were unsure if it would be sold and torn down. Now, that their future is secure, he said, improvements are on the way. The location is perfect; steps to the beach, a variety of stores and restaurants across the street, and public transit at the front door. It is definately not a Hilton or a Sheraton but for the price, well worth it. We will be going back.Mona  Maurice RoyToronto, Ontario

reviews.author: Mylene Deckow PhD
reviews.content: My favorite part & service of this stay was the bellmen & valet service. Gustavo & Sebastian were superb with their service, direction and hospitality. Not a bad location but a car is necessary to explore Miami. Our 2 bedroom apt was fine. The buffet breakfast included in our package was limited and the hot food warm at best. Would most likely not stay again.

title: Sonoma

description: Registered on the National Register of Historic Places, Swiss Hotel is in a great location on the Plaza. A small group of guest rooms overlook the Plaza above an Italian restaurant and bar. Located in the heart of the town and near several bars, the inn can attract street noise in the late evenings, including rowdy tourists heading back to their hotels after a long day (and night) of wine country drinking.
name: The Swiss Hotel

reviews.author: Marilyne Romaguera
reviews.content: Fortune House is a condo hotel. Furniture is like dorm-room style with a couch that was on a metal frame with stained removable cushions. No amenities. No services. No wireless Internet in the rooms (only the lobby). Hotel was relatively safe and room was clean. Bed was cheap. No TV in the bedroom -- only the living room. Would not want to stay there again.

reviews.author: Aliza Rau
reviews.content: We didn't get the room that we requested but we did get a room just as nice. They didn't tell us we were getting a different room, they just hoped we wouldn't complain. I requested a 2-room condo with 4 queen beds, I was given a 2-room condo with one queen bed and 2 sets of bunk beds. Good thing it was just a bunch of young college friends and not older couples; twin bunk beds don't usually work for older couples. Most of the hallways were under construction. The rooms in each condo were on the small side as well as the kitchen. The living room was nice with leather couches and a balcony. Dining room had pretty chairs. Be careful when walking out to the balcony though the sliding glass door has a 3inch lip that i tripped on walking out to the balcony. I don't know if that is on all rooms, but it was in mine.

title: Sonoma

description: A contemporary resort and spa located just south of Sonoma Plaza. Beautiful and super modern, with a big pool spotted with cabanas, a great place to nurse a hangover after wine tasting, or to cater to your family when the kids just want to have fun. Pets are allowed with a charge, and internet is additional. They have two restaurants.
name: The Lodge at Sonoma Renaissance Resort & Spa

reviews.author: Cloyd Ernser
reviews.content: Just right! I stayed here during a conference for 3 nights during Feb 2005, paying $145 a night. The hotel is stylish and quaint and tastefully decorated in Deco style. the lobby is nicely appointed with several reading areas and designer mags for guests. The rooms were elegantly simple. I appreciated that the TV was ceiling mounted to maximize floor space with pre-fixed max volume control levels to keep sound levels down between rooms. the walkin closet too is a nice feature!!! The bathrooms were spartan, decorated in white and very very clean!! The bed was great, and the four pillows, were outstanding!!!!!I did not have the chance to test the small pool, but it nonetheless looked clean and inviting. My only complaint was that another group of guests made an extraordinary amount of noise coming and going each morn and evening, it sounded as if they were moving crates of metal poles. Turns out they were a production crew for some resident supermodels that are regulars...i guess the noise was not that bad....I will come back here again....

reviews.author: Queen Green
reviews.content: Great location if you love Lincoln Rd. food and shops   showReview(1704682, 'full');

reviews.author: Rey Koch
reviews.content: If I could give a negative star I would. This place was the worst hotel experience I have had in my life. The sheets were dirty, the carpet was in worse shape I didn't want to walk barefoot in the room for fear of getting fungus, but worst of all is the fact that I left some very important items in the room that were stolen. If you value your possessions don't stay here!!!

reviews.author: Lonzo Walter
reviews.content: Simple, clean hotel with very big room with clean bed linen. The hotel is in a short driving distance from the airport and has very good price as it seems that airport hotel close to Miami are expensive. Internet in the room was not working (or very very slow) that was the negative point about this hotel. The breakfast is really good, with a lot of options, good service. I would recommend this hotel for the prices compared to other structures in the area, cleanness of the room and the complimentary breakfast.

reviews.author: Orrin Hills
reviews.content: Stayed here twice in a week due to location. Never felt valued by staff whatsoever. Arrived to airport, rang hotel many times but took roughly 20mins to answer the phone...beware of answering machine. Tranfers were good if you are prepared to wait. On my first visit i left a hooded sweater and headphones in the room, rang straight away after leaving and was told they would be kept for me. On returning, the items were apparently never found. Reception staff were too interesting in texting on their phones than addressing my issue, two staff members grunted at me as we waited for airport transfers...not good enough. As expected, property was never returned. Breakfast was a nightmare...no seats and food was not re stocked after 9.15. When i asked for more bread and butter, i was greeted with a look of dismay from a staff member. Better off bringing breakfast back to your room. Room was fine as was cleanliness, just feel cheated out of my hard earned money. Would appreciate some sort of response to ronanperry@hotmail.com and would like to see some customer service in operation.

reviews.author: Mr. Nikko Hudson
reviews.content: I got an excellent rate for this hotel (around 90 dollars per night) so it was hard to resist even after reading all the reviews on this site, I still put my trust on the fact that is a Marriott and I am very glad I did. My experience in this hotel was good, and I didn't give it 5 points just because I do believe they should updgrade the floors and carpets in the rooms and add a little more "wow" factor to the decoration, having said this, the rest of my experience for the 5 nights I stayed was pretty good. These are my observations: 1. The lobby and front area of the hotel is inviting, nicely decorated and has a lot of nice functional spaces to hold meetings with colleagues or other guests. 2. There is a station that has access to coffee to go and water with lime on that area that I found super convenient. 3. Most of the staff (not everyone) were friendly and helpful 4. The courtyard with the swimming pool was actually really nice and enjoyable both, at day or at night. 5. The rooms were really spacious and the beds are very comfortable. 6. The furniture on the rooms is actually really nice (except for the dated sofa) but there is a large HD TV and it is well layed out. 7. Closet space and drawer space are enough and convenient. 8. For me, Internet connectivity is really important, and this hotel had one of the best I have seen in a hotel so far, it was fast and simple. 9. Even when I got a room on the first floor due to my rate, the rooms were reasonably quiet (this is an airport hotel) On the not so great side of things: 1. The carpets need to go or to be changed 2. The decoration in some areas needs to be re-vamped. 3. The excercise room wasnt all that great 4. The breakfast buffett wasn't all that great, so, I ended up just once and went to Denny's (1 minute drive from the hotel) for Breakfast the rest of my time. So, in the end a pleasant experience, and it definitely surprased my initial expectations and I would recommend, specially when it helped me save over half of my bill. Cheers!

reviews.author: Kiley Steuber
reviews.content: I just recently stayed here for an overnight stay. First off, I did not hear any noise even though the airport is nearby. The beds were pretty comfortable and are good for a good nights rest. The rooms are ok, not like the newer Courtyard hotels. It did have a big flat screen TV. But some parts of the room looked a bit dated. Some of the desk agents were not wearing name tags which is a Marriott standard. The hotel itself is ok and I would stay there again if in the area.

reviews.author: Gus Champlin
reviews.content: Nice hotel. Recently renovated. Staff nice. Nice business area. Restaurant onsite. Rather hard to find from Miami Airport - lots of road construction making finding hotel difficult if driving.

reviews.author: Mr. Arianna Stark
reviews.content: We were at Lake Eve in July 2011 2adults 3 kids excellent suite, all comforts of home all staff very friendly nice bar, pool was great for kids. Lake eve is on bottom of I Drive so quiet but with I trolley stops at lake eve is handy to all attractions, there is a handy Publix department store just 2 stops south on I trolley so we were able to do 2 weeks in Orlando Florida on I drive without the hassle of car rental. So no worries about drink driving or getting lost we had a ball. Would definitely go back to Lake Eve.

title: Sonoma

description: Luxury hotel property with a spa. Western themed, but don't let that square you - the decor is modern and classy with all the touches of a (fancy) home. Lovely gardens, many types of rooms, and free internet. Breakfast and wine reception included. MacArthur also has free DVDs you can borrow and a fitness center. Onsite restaurant is Saddles, a high end steakhouse with a tacky cowboy theme.
name: MacArthur Place

reviews.author: Mr. Ericka Stehr
reviews.content: We stayed here from 17th dec2011 till 31st dec 2011. We were suprised by the space of the apartments. you get a fully equiped kitchen.THE 2 bedrooms were of good size wthe comfy beds. in the master the jaccuzi bath was great along with the walkin shower. fabulous. The hotel staff were pleasent. the only downside is the sun doesnt go over the pool area.( But the pool was heated to perfection and the hot tub was great. Kids loved the pool. We stayed on the 8th floor and the view was lovely. when we visit Florida again we will defo stay at Lake Eve.

title: Sonoma

description: Four quaint cottages owned by the folks who own Sonoma's Best, a local general store. About a 30-minute walk from downtown Sonoma, this is a great launching point to check out Sonoma Valley and visit Napa. Romantic with private patios. Free internet.
name: Sonoma's Best Guest Cottages

reviews.author: Marie Erdman
reviews.content: We chose this hotel because of location and it was perfect for what we wanted close to restaurants shops + attractions. The apartment was lovely much bigger than we expected with a full kitchen + washer dryer facilities which we did make use of. The bathroom was great with jaccuzi bath + large walk in shower with a second bathroom that we did not use. Bed huge + comfortable we had great view over looking lake + pool and spent time on balcony with nice glass of wine to relax after long hot day. Ate in Restaurant once lovely steak meal + very reasonable used pool + hot tub in the evening which was great after tiring days in themeparks. apartment kept spotless already planning to return 2012 with family cant wait....

reviews.author: Courtney Klein
reviews.content: Myself and my family have visited Florida many times, usually staying in villas. This time we decided to stay in an apartment and definitely made the right choice with Lake Eve. The apartments are just as comfortable as staying in a villa, with all the facilities you could possibly need. The beds were all large and very comfortable, and all three bedrooms had a lovely view of the lake. Both bathrooms were nice, the en-suite was very smart with a separate shower, twin sinks and a Jacuzzi bath! The kitchen was well equipped with a washing machine, dryer, large fridge etc . The only difference we found between the apartment and a villa was of course the shared pool, this was more than compensated for by the wonderful view from the balcony.... of the lake by day, and the lights of Orlando by night! The pool area itself was plenty big enough and there were always sunbeds available, the sun does go off this area late afternoon, but to be honest it was so hot we were quite happy by then to have some shade. The position of Lake Eve is excellent, this end of International Drive is quieter which is lovely after a day at the parks, but is within walking distance (10-15 minutes) or 5 minutes by bus, to a Publix where you can get any groceries you need, a McDonalds, Dominoes Pizza, a couple of other restaurants and The Premium Outlet shopping mall. The apartment was clean, the cleaners coming everyday was also a bonus and they even put the dishwasher on if we had gone out in a hurry and left some crockery out! , Any staff we met were very friendly and helpful, especially the young barman who we chatted to while waiting in the bar for our taxi to arrive. We were very impressed and will definitely stay here again.

reviews.author: Vaughn Zulauf
reviews.content: I found this hotel on Hotwire for approx. $100/night â€“ a great find for a two bedroom/two bathroom condo. This hotel is comparable to Vistana Village (which is right across the street). It is in a great location if you are going to Disney. The hotel appeared to be new and our room was very nice. It had new appliances, furniture etc. The bathrooms were very large â€“ loved the Jacuzzi tub! The patio was also great â€“ we could see fireworks at night. We stayed on July 4th so the hotel was a little noisy as I am sure most hotels were that night. Our only complaint would be that the wait for the elevator was a little long every time we needed to use it. Also, Iâ€™m not sure why valet parking is offered for the small parking lot, but you donâ€™t have to use it. It was a great stay. We will be back.

reviews.author: Lamont Baumbach
reviews.content: I stayed at ths hotel with my husband and my 3 year old daughter in May 2011 and would come back again certainly. It is a very new hotel and so all the rooms are very new, well decorated, spacious and confortable. There's a big swimming pool and an astonishing and relaxing lake view. The staff of the hotel is very friendly too and it's very well located, close to a big Publix and a Walgreens, close to Disney parks, Sea World and Premium Outlet. I do recommend it.

reviews.author: Lonny Brekke
reviews.content: Waited at front desk 10-15 minutes before someone acknowledged presence, couldn't check in but we were there at around noon which was before check in time, so we returned later. Then we discovered that microwave was missing the turntable, so called and someone came up a while later, and when turned stove on, there was white smoke coming out so we turned it off and decided that we probably shouldn't use it. We also had bed sheets which were stained and had cigarette burns. Also a couple of towels had stains on them, and finally the door to the balcony didn't have a screen door so when you kept it open for a nice Florida breeze, bugs would fly in. Definately not impressed and would suggest you think twice.

reviews.author: Loren Bernhard
reviews.content: I booked 3, two bedroom suites back in November for a conference that my colleagues and I were attending. The day before we left I emailed to confirm our reservation, I received an email confirmation. The day we left one of my colleagues called to make sure that the hotel knew of our late check-in (our plane did not arrive until 11:30 pm). Upon arriving at the hotel, by this time it was past midnight, we were told that check-in was at 3:00 pm and since we were not there at the allotted time they had given our rooms away. Completely unacceptable! Needless to say we were very upset considering the difficulty we had in locating new rooms during the spring break in Orlando at 12:30 am. We were assured by the man/boy working the desk that our rooms were only given away for the night, and that the rest of our reservation was secured for the next day. Some of our team had to scramble to find another hotel for the night while I had to share 1 bedroom with 3 work colleagues. During this period the night desk person was very rude to us. The next day we tried to confirm the rest of our stay, and the day desk person told us that the entire reservation was given to someone else. Unbelieveable! We had booked for 5 days to attend a work function and were now homeless. The person at the desk actually said to my boss "too bad, so sad" The staff at this hotel are the most unprofessional that I have ever encountered. I'm sure they gave our rooms away to the first people that walked in off the street offering a kick back to them. I will never stay at this hotel again, nor do I recommend it to anyone.

reviews.author: Linnea White
reviews.content: I just stayed at Lake Eve Resort last week and it was Amazing. We are a family of 7 adults and we stayed in the 2 bedroom room w/kitchen etc. This worked so well for our family. We were in Orlando visiting all the parks. It was nice to come back at the end of the day and be able to relax. The hotel was very clean, the employees were very curtious. And it was just great. I would definently recommend staying here if you are visiting Orlando for an extended stay. We will definently stay here if and when we return to Orlando.

reviews.author: Ashlee Champlin
reviews.content: Resort was very nice. 2-bedroom suites were amazing: full kitchen, dining room with table, living room, 2 bedrooms, 2 baths, balcony, washer/dryer. Master bathroom was huge (jetted tub and separate tiled shower). 3 big screen TV's with nice channel selection. Didn't have to go get ice down the hall because refrigerator had water and ice dispenser. Large swimming pool with kiddie pool and hot tub. Free wi-fi was very fast. Not much of a business center (2 computers in the hallway). Charged for internet use, so went to room and used free wi-fi. We got a great last minute rate. Much cheaper than staying on Disney property. They have complimentary shuttle service to Epcot (3 times a day). Resort is not far from the Disney parks and Universal Studios. Many restaurants and grocery store close to hotel. Did not try hotel restaurant. We really enjoyed our stay. Great value for the money!

reviews.author: Janis Kozey
reviews.content: Stayed here for 2 weeks on the 14th floor, and received excellent service from polite and informative staff. The 2 bedroomed apartment was clean,spacious, comfortable, and well appointed ,with a beautiful view. Centrally located, it was handy for supermarket(Publix), Outlet Mall(Premier), Restaurants ,and of course the Parks. The Trolleyline came in handy a few times too.The hotel amenities were very good (pool,fitness centre etc).Provision of electric jug/kettle would have been appreciated for cups of tea(,a coffee perc is provided ). The large screen LCD TV's in each room were well used.All in all a very very comfortable and enjoyable stay. We would certainly stay here again.

title: Sonoma

description: One of the first of an emerging chain by the man behind the Malmaison and Hotel du Vin groups. Has already gathered numerous plaudits.
name: Dakota Forth Bridge
title: South Queensferry

description: Good bar meals in the evening.
name: Lochboisdale Hotel
title: South Uist

description: Handy for the ferry and the bus station.
name: Caladh Inn

reviews.author: Miss Helmer Wisozk
reviews.content: If you are sound sensitive this is not the place for you, if you dont mind the noise its not a bad place, could do better , could do worrse. I checked in and asked for a quiet room was pleased to get an end room with a nice view, but it was right above very loud road construction and later had to endure very noisey drunks in bar on mezzanine right below me, I was told they would quiet down at 7pm, than 730 than maybe 930..was offered different rooms but all of the rooms had a very very noisey HVAC system that had a constant hummmm that was very annoying. I know from previous stays in nearby hotels the airplane noise is worse at other end Also was expecting a nice balcony but in reality it is only maybe 18" wide, just enough to stand there. The good: Very good front desk, especially the women on 2nd shift (Silver?) decent bathroom, good bed, nice view, quick shuttle to the strip, room was fresh and not too heavy on air freshener like some places. The bad: Road traffic noise, road construction noise, airplane noise, noisey hum in room from hvac, noisey bar patrons in atrium, food and service in restaurant need some improvement, plates for breakfast buffet dirty, fitness center disapointing, two long black hairs in bathroom sink that were not mine, slight cigarette odor coming from bathroom vent

reviews.author: Jordane Medhurst
reviews.content: We don't normally like to stay in Las Vegas but since it is half way to our destination we chose to stay there. We are not into the Las Vegas scene at all so wanted a hotel without a casino. We have 5 children and were looking for something affordable. We came across Embassy Suites on Priceline and for a family of seven it worked great. The room had a living room/sm. kitchenette area and then a separate bedroom. The room would sleep 6 and we put the baby in his own playpen and also had the staff bring up a portable single bed. The kids loved the pool and their favorite part (and mine) was the made to order breakfast in the morning that was included in the price of the room. That kind of breakfast would have easily cost us $40 at a restaurant. We were cozy in the place but it certainly worked for the one night we stayed and was clean and comfortable and the staff was helpful and the hotel was very nice. The kids loved the glass elevators as well.

reviews.author: Albina Kiehn
reviews.content: I was a little disappointed with this hotel. The rooms very outdated and look dirty. It's hard to tell if anything is clean as the decor is so dark. Our bedding had holes. No free WiFi, no flat screen tv's. It was okay for a night, but I would hate to spend a vacation at this hotel.

reviews.author: Geo Boehm
reviews.content: This hotel is off the strip and offers the sensitive traveler a quiet respite. Check in was impeccable and swift, the room was clean and comfortable. Breakfast was delicious. The only disappointment was my call to the Front Desk for more towels was either forgotten or ignored. The hotel is located behind the Wynn and it's about a mile walk to the Wynn entrance. There is a Las Vegas Monorail station a 5 minute walk away.

title: Stornoway

description: Spacious detached property which sleeps 8 in comfort. Wales Tourist Board 3*. Children and pets are most welcome. Short breaks and longer stays available.
name: Gower Edge Self Catering

reviews.author: Kareem Schamberger
reviews.content: Stayed at the Comfort Inn from 18 - 21 Feb 2005. The advantages of this hotel are it's excellent location, it's a stone throw from Macy's and the Empire State was right next to our bedroom window. It is also only a 10 minute walk to Times Square. It has a nice bar next door called Brendan's where you can get the british footie results. However, on the negative, our room reaked of cigarette smoke, and even though we are both smokers it was unbearable. The hotel couldn't give us another room and despite them promising to elimate the odour and us purchasing air freshener, it did not go away. Also the receptionist Alex was abrupt to the point of being rude on some occasions, and the heating was roasting hot and very noisy. The breakfast was ok, just cereal, bagels, toast & doughnuts etc. But it's worth going to a diner for the real american breakfast experience. Try the Empire in Chelsea it's fab. I would recommend this hotel if you are on a budget but request a non-smoking room and hope that you get one as some people checked in when we were checking out and had requested this but ended up with our old room - poor them!

reviews.author: Neil Berge
reviews.content: We stayed for five nights in June 2004, encouraged by all the positive reviews the hotel had previously received. We were delighted to find those reviews were very accurate in respect of the excellent value for money, helpfulness of the staff and the close proximity to a lot of the main attractions. As a base for a sightseeing trip, we are sure it will be very hard to beat. Our room was clean and tidy, good sized and, being at the rear of the hotel, very quiet. The buffet breakfast was adequate enough, but we didn't choose it for the cuisine. As a budget hotel we would certainly recommend it to anyone visiting the city for a few days.

reviews.author: Hilda Prohaska
reviews.content: We had a one bedroom suite, spacious and modern. Nice view, nice interior and nice staff. Location ok, several restaurants and diners nearby, short distance to bus and subway.

reviews.author: Evie Kovacek
reviews.content: The staff is very helpful and nice. Always a smile and do you need anything if you approached the desk. You will not feel like you are staying in a hotel but like you live in NYC and this is your residence. We had a 3 bedroom 3 bath for X-mas week. The place was awesome with so much space because the bedrooms were big and the bathrooms. The best, you had a full kitchen and could make a breakfast before heading out which saves you money. The subway was a quick walk and its nice to get lost the best part and when you learn the routes you definitely feel like you live there. The Taxi rides were never over 20 (was holiday so a lot of traffic) and if you take the subway just take a Taxi back. Overall, if you want to experience NYC you don't need a fancy place because the time you spend in a room is only for sleep. However, this place is a clean, spacious, and attentive place that's inexpensive place to stay. Now, I would recommend a little TLC because it does show a little use the rooms like paint and new decor. Yet, for the time we were in the room it was nothing that bothered. Stay here if you want to experience living there, have a big group, and like space not the little hotel room.

reviews.author: Laverna McKenzie
reviews.content: If you are looking for a comfortable serviced apartment in a quieter area of Manhattan, this may be for you. The suites are large with well-equipped kitchens and a small balcony. For the size of the rooms, the price is pretty reasonable. The decor was a little faded, but the concierge is helpful and when you're in New York, you don't have to spend a lot of time indoors!

reviews.author: Arnaldo Blanda PhD
reviews.content: The brief to find accommodation suitable for 6 women for a week was proving difficult until we stumpbled across Marmara. 3 large bedrooms - we were willing to share with 2 king size beds and 2 singles - 3 bathrooms a great living area and fantastic 27th floor views. There was also a washer and dryer in the apartment which really made us feel at home. Some habits die hard! The location was fine with a good subway line close and with grocery and grog shops nearby we were set. The staff at reception were extremely helpful to us. They were happy to suggest places to shop, sightsee and eat. They also printed out some travel instructions to help us get to Brooklyn which made it easy and in general were accommodating of any request we made - thanks to all the staff who looked after us. It was great to have free WiFi although the connection was a little tempremental while we were there - not sure if this is usual. Would recommend Marmara to anyone who wanted to feel more at home than a hotel room can provide. Would also recommend Nicks Italian across the road - great food, great friendly service.

title: Swansea

description: Hurst Dene is located in the leafy suburb of Uplands just off Uplands Square on the main road to the heart of the Gower peninsula. They offer guest rooms and self-catering apartments at affordable prices.
name: Hurst Dene Guest House

reviews.author: Alvina Tremblay
reviews.content: We met a really serviceminded personnel, this is always a good start for a stay! Further on we got a really nice room - an appartment really - on 19th floor, and the view was spectacular! Walking south on 2nd Ave you find breakfast and dinner easily. Nearness of Central Park for fresh air, quick access to museums, and short way to Lexington Ave for shopping makes this hotel a really good starting point. If one doesn't want all the walking we did, getting on buses is no problem, and Subway is only some blocks away. We slept really well in comfortable beds. The kitchenette was well equipped - with fridge, freezer and cooking possibilities. Please note that we upgraded the room as we wanted to stay as high up as possible and this we find was well spent money. I really would recommend this hotel for other travellers when purpose is exploring NYC.

reviews.author: Wanda Mohr
reviews.content: We came to new york city for the holidays, and we stayed at The Marmara Manhattan and it was great. thank you for your hospitality. Special thanks to Mr.Mete ,Mr.Mehmet and all the other staff. We recommend everybody who would come to New York.

reviews.author: Randy Ferry
reviews.content: We had booked a studio at this apartment hotel and got a one bedroom suite. It was very roomy, clean and comftorable. There is alot of construction going on outside but that did not really bother us. It is probably as far north as you want to go but we did feel safe. There is an all night pizza place and a little grocery right across the street. It is just a short walk to Lexington where there are at least a million restaurants! You are close to bus and subway stops. On the subway you are not more than 15 minutes to most places including Times Square and the Theatre District. Otherwise you can walk to Central Park , alot ol the museums, restaurants and some shopping. The staff was very nice. All that said we will look for another location. It is a little out of the way and as I said earlier the location is not the best but, if you are looking for alot of room in a clean space this is great.

reviews.author: Seth Predovic
reviews.content: My husband and our 2 kids (1&2 yr olds) luckily used our credit card reward points to book a 3 night stay and paid for a 2 br upgrade and we are so happy with our pick. The room is spacious and clean and great for traveling with toddlers. The staff is extremely friendly and answered every question professionally. With every good thing there's always a few cons. The rooms are quite a bit outdated and in need of renovation (old old fixtures, door handles are loose and furnishings are different from the pics). The other think to be cautious of is if you're traveling with toddlers, there are alot of electrical wires from the printer and modem that are dangerous. But all in all, were happy with the space. Room to breathe!

reviews.author: Lurline Trantow
reviews.content: I strongly recommend the Marmara Manhattan. My husband was an in-patient at Mt. Sinai Hospital and we did not know the duration of his stay. I am a small town girl and intimidated by staying in New Your City by myself. The Marmara Manhattan became my haven. Nesli (and the others in reservations) are wonderful. They understand that surgical and medical stays are unpredictable in duration and very stressful. They provided us a great rate because my husband was in-patient at Mt. Sinai and were very flexible in scheduling. The hotel is very attractive, clean, roomy, and well maintained. It is unique, not your standard cookie cutter rooms. It is about a 20 minute walk to Mt. Sinai which I enjoyed taking. The suites are really attractive and even my teenage daughters were saying what I good job I had done finding it! In addition to the great rate, it has a full kitchen and a grocery around the corner. I cooked most meals there and was able to keep down the cost of my stay. What particularly struck me about the Marmara Manhattan is that everyone from reservations, to the front desk, to the doorman and bellhop, were friendly and helpful. This place understands customer service. Unfortunately we will not be coming back to the Marmara because my husband has chosen to change hospitals to Cleveland Clinic. When he is all fixed up and healthy again, my goal is to return to the Marmara for a few days with my family and relax and spend some time with them in the city. If you need to find a place while a family member is in one of the nearby hospitals, choose the Marmara Manhattan. I talked to other families who had chosen the Wales and the Marriot. They were not nearly as happy with their choices as I was with mine.

title: Swansea

description: 2 self catering cottages,  sleeping 6 and 4-5 respectively.  Located in a very quiet hamlet, above the Swansea and Neath Valleys.  4* graded cottages, free Wi Fi, off road parking, cycle storage.  15 minutes from the M4 at Neath or Swansea.
directions: Easy access from the M4, Neath / Swansea
name: Tan yr Eglwys Cottages

reviews.author: Roel Konopelski
reviews.content: Upon most encounters with slovenly appearances, most would amend, fearing the thrifty and gaping caverns of dues to be paid. However, there is a crossing point: precisely where this slum seemed to have leaped off the pylons of great hotels, creating a tourist-friendly New York stay into paralytic asphyxiation of horror. It looms precariously on it's two-star rating. The distraught condition of drug predators and illegals cramming their holed guts into the trickling walls probably purchased the minimal rating. To this corroded stature of suckitude, I may ask to command you a simple trot away, scorchingly applying pressure to the gas pedal. First off, the "lobby" is a tight and small space... just like the stairway/room/elevator. Our room had no form of heating in the brutal cold of Manhattan. The bathroom door did not lock, "great". The bed was FILTHY!! STAINS! STAINS! HAIR ON THE BED! My goodness... there is no standards for this dump. The doors have stains... i don't know what it is? Mold? Feces? Basically, the room we had checked in online for about half a week was canceled the first hour we were there and the whole hour we were there was trying to book another hotel.

reviews.author: Aidan Kuhlman
reviews.content: Wow, the reviews are so bad, I feel like I need to stand up for this place. Look, it's not the Four Seasons, but for the price, it's really a decent place. We were close to everything, from sightseeing to shopping to dinning to the broadway shows. Rooms were small, but clean. The front desk agents there are super friendly and having wifi helped. So my friends and I had a good time and saved a bunch, so it was all good.

reviews.author: Manuel Hudson V
reviews.content: better to sleep on the street you do not have a lobby and extra charges for everything if been there once you will never come back there

reviews.author: Camden Durgan
reviews.content: I booked this hotel to be next to the place my girlfriend was interviewing at. We walked into the room and wanted to vomit. There were worn spots and stains on the sheets and urine staining (clearly from decades of men missing the toilet) on the bathroom floor, and a spongelike mold growing out of the "drain" of the "jet" tub. It was absolutely disgusting. AND...even though we walked in and walked right back out.....they would NOT refund our money. It was worth it to call hotels.com and find a great place at Broadway Plaza Hotel for only $10.00 more a night. I would stay very very far away. Bed bugs, urine, and who knows what other things lurk here. I've stayed in placed with cockroaches and ants crawling the walls that were better than this place.....

reviews.author: Dr. Kay Bednar
reviews.content: Finally I found somewhere I could write a review about this place to save others our pain. I see now on their website they have renovated somewhat. WE were NOT told at the time of our booking they were in the middle of renovations. Courtesy would at least grant that and give us the choice. Hopefully they have changed their ways but after reading latest reviews perhaps not. Towels were dirty, bathroom floor was dirty and room was dirty. I had to clean the bath before I could use it. Bed was hard, bedding disgusting. Glad it was a brief trip. They tried to put us in a room that had just been painted and the window didn't open at first. Then we asked for another room which had patches of carpet on the floor. Forget this hotel if you are light sleeper, tile floors line the hallways and suitcases rolling on the floors are noisy as is anyone walking by. We were on a tight budget and had to be in NYC that day. For around $170 at the time what a rip off!!!! I will go without food and save money to stay in a decent place and thankfully we have since found other hotels better quality for a cheaper price. Oh and yes there is no elevator and if they didn't have my credit card number I would have left on arrival after seeing the foyer!

reviews.author: Rita Jacobi
reviews.content: The other reviews scared me, but I already booked this place before I read them. I don't know about all the other rooms, but our room was pretty nice (not huge), but was very comfortable. I requested a room with two queen beds and not only got 2 queens, but two singles as well! Really cool! The T.V. was small and not very many channels, but hey, we weren't there to watch T.V.! Great location. Nice folks working there. If you go there, request room 309. That was ours. Not bad at all.

reviews.author: Dejuan Feil
reviews.content: Was expecting hotel to be poor if not terrible after reading reviews on trip advisor. Was not prepared for what we discovered. Nobody even heard of the hotel, bugs in the rooms, rooms not cleaned, leaking toilet, very noisy etc and the receptionist advised us we were getting the best room in the hotel. A joke ! Don`t know how they are even allowes keep this place opened. Awful !!!! We spent very little time in the hotel but still would not dream of reccomending it what so ever. Worst hotel I have ever come accross !!!!!!!!!!!! Don`t book it !!!

reviews.author: Rosemary Hessel
reviews.content: Our recent stay to this hotel was like going camping. Someone else booked this room for us. First of all we got there in the evening. We were not sure at first if we were in the right place because when the cab driver dropped us off we thought we were walking through the back door of a restaurant. We checked in took the elevator, which only held 3 small people to our room. The hall itself was so narrow two people had to take turns walking down them. Our room had no air on upon arrival, had to open the window to plug in the AC. In the room we had to take turns walking around because it was so small. We found smashed roaches on the wall, no soap, toilet lid, shampoo, no place to put toiletries in bathroom, had to ask for towels, looks to be like they had water leaks down the walls, had a dressing table but could not use it because the stool was between the table & bed and you could not pull out the stool. Make sure if you book here that your clothes are pressed unless you like ironing out in the hallway on attached to wall iron & board. NIGHTMARE!

reviews.author: Jesse Kessler
reviews.content: This hotel was the worst hotel I ever stayed in. The room was dull and cramped. The sheets were dirty and the walls stained. The staff entered our room while we were out shopping and moved us to another room. The packed our belongings in bags and moved us from two to one claiming there was a plumming problem but I demanded to see our old room to discover it was already let to new people. We had to call the police to sort the problem out he said it was a bad hotel and we should not have been there. Then on our last night I was confronted by a huge mouse in the lobby!!! The hotel manager was abusive and hung up the phone when we complained. Don't stay here. Lisa

title: Swansea

description: Hurst Dene is located in the leafy suburb of Uplands just off Uplands Square on the main road to the heart of the Gower peninsula. They offer guest rooms and self-catering apartments at affordable prices.
name: Hurst Dene Guest House

reviews.author: Celestine Keeling
reviews.content: We used Hotels to find a newyorkcity hotel. when we arrived it was like a bowery flop house! I had my children with me and we had to get two rooms and they werent even next to each other. There were broken bottles in the hall and pubic hairs in the bed. this was the broadwaymanhattan hotel. we were going to see a show and wanted to walk in the theater district. I couldnt stay there with my family, so we had to get another room at the hilton, which cost us 400. for the night, plus the 200 that was non refundable from the other place! We contacted Hotels that we booked through and they took no responsibility and wouldnt even discuss what we had found at this hotel that gets 2 stars on their site! Beware of the catchy Hotels commercials, we learned the hard way.

reviews.author: Mrs. Yolanda Emmerich
reviews.content: I would not stay here even if they pay me. The WORST hotel at which I've ever stayed. It's 10 times worse than what you can possibly imagine of a bad hotel. It's so very nasty. I couldn't stand it even with my eyes closed.

reviews.author: Mrs. Ellsworth VonRueden
reviews.content: My friend, her parents and I wanted to be able to go to New York and have a good time, but when we all saw this hotel, we knew what a dump it was. Just that the fact that there is a piece of paper taped to the door to tell you that this is a hotel should give you an idea about how bad it really is...No room, screws for sink handles, clogged bathtub drain. The TV didn't work, there was no VCR, you could hear everything that was going on in the rooms around you. The elevator smelled horrible and the curtains didn't even keep out the light. Lastly, there were no dressers, just hangers and hooks, that's it! Yes, the hotel IS located close to times square but is it worth it to stay somewhere that is totally disgusting just to be close to times square?

reviews.author: Ms. Thaddeus McDermott
reviews.content: I took my daughter and three of her friends here for a girls weekend. The room and bathroom were very clean and the staff was very helpful. The only problem (and to five females it was a big one) and surprise was that we saw several cockroaches!! I was very surprised as it all looked very clean. I think the attached restaraunt (a small chinese diner style place) is the culprit here. We all had trouble sleeping because of this, thank god we were only there for two nights and were not in the room except to sleep and shower. Ths day staff was great and we had plenty of heat once we asked for it. I have to say if it weren't for the bugs I would recommend this hotel but I will never go back, I hate bugs!!!!!

reviews.author: Ms. Jackson Stark
reviews.content: stayed for a week, older hotel, but clean rooms and very friendly staff, breakfast included in price and was very good. very good value for the money. staff was very helpful in getting our truck in the parkade, which was a close fit. beds were comfy. will definately be staying here again.

reviews.author: Dr. Hortense Ebert
reviews.content: We stayed at the Quality Inn for one night precruise. Our room was small but clean,and the beds were very comfortable with nice linens. Ice machines are on alternate floors. I would like to recognize Jacob at the front desk for providing excellent service. He gave us directions to the Aquabus to Granville Island,and also arranged a taxi at a specified time for our group to the cruise port. I see from other reviews that there were complaints about the restaurant in the hotel. We did not avail ourselves of the hotel restaurant. We ate dinner that night and breakfast the next morning at the White Spot restaurant,which is one block east of the Quality Inn(see review).

title: Swansea

description: Hotel just outside Swindon with fully equipped gym and 23 metre indoor swimming pool, as well as health and beauty spa. Special Spa Break from £75.00 per person for one night including three course dinner, full English breakfast and choice of beauty treatment.
directions: from Exit M4 (J15) and follow A419 to Cirencester, straight over roundabout, then take exit on left signposted Oxford (A420), turn right at next two roundabouts, stay on A420, cross the roundabout, pass the police station on left, pass the Esso garage on left, hotel is next turning on left, signposted to South Marston
name: Mercure South Marston Hotel

reviews.author: Lexus Jakubowski
reviews.content: The hotel was well situated in the financial district. Staff was incredibly friendly and even the cleaning staff would greet you. The lobby was small but well appointed. Rooms were good although the glass door on the bathroom did not lend for privacy on a family trip. Hotel has 3 small cardio areas and a good reading room that serves as a bar on weekends. Rates appear to be steep but you can find good weekend rates. Close to South Street Seaport and other financial district tourist attractions, as well as Chinatown.

reviews.author: Nelle Hammes
reviews.content: The Gild Hall is really nice. Luxurious, comfortable, quiet, and close to the public transportation. The employees are very friendly and do their best to accommodate you in anyway possible. My room looked really nice, its had very interesting decor, check out the website to see what I am talking about. Its like a modern retro kind of feel, I can't explain it, but I loved it. My room had an iPod dock, bath robes, and all the fancy stuff hotels provide. The only issue I had was my room phone didn't work so I had to use my cell phone to make calls. Other than that my room was spectacular. My stay over all could've been better. I went to the Libertine Restaurant which is a part of Gild Hall and I wish I didn't. I called them every 10 minutes for about an hour before I planned on arriving and no one ever picked up the phone. When I finally went down I was told there was a 25min wait. Which I could've avoided if they picked up the phone. So after the 25min wait turned into 40min, I was upset and went for a walk. walking past the restaurant window I noticed there were three open tables. At this point I wanted to leave but my fiance insisted we stay; so we stayed. The manager seemed nice, but the waitress made us feel like she had better things to do with her time. The food was great though. I'm a sucker for good food, so I kinda forgot about the horrible service when I started eating. So to sum it up: Gild Hall is great stay, but beware of the Libertine.

reviews.author: Hermann Adams
reviews.content: Gild Hall really impressed when my girlfriend and I stayed there last month. The service was great, room was spacious and bed was super comfortable. This hotel is located in the heart of the financial district, but succeeded in making us feel like we were at home. It was convenient for all of the trains b/c they all basically converge on Wall St and Fulton St. Far and above better than a standard chain like the Hilton or Hyatt and a great deal, too. Highly recommended - we'll be staying there next time we're in NYC.

title: Swindon

description: Comfortable hotel just outside Tamworth town centre with Reuben's Restaurant & Bar.  Ideal for business guests with free wifi and easy access to motorway network.  Very family friendly with excellent value packages including entry to Drayton Manor & Thomas Land, Conkers, Twycross Zoo and National Space Centre.
name: Ramada Tamworth

reviews.author: Lera Stoltenberg I
reviews.content: I want to commend Hotel Le Marais on their hospitality, competence, reliability, efficiency and attentiveness regarding our hotel stay. From the beginning of our booking process earlier this year to the final checkout I have been very pleased with all stages and personnel. Shawn was excellent booking my room and giving me the room I requested. He was always cheerful and anxious to help The next day after we checked in, we overslept for breakfast and we came down to the front desk and explained that we have missed breakfast this morning and asked to the young lady who was working if we could get some fruit and yogurt. As fast as she could she ran to the kitchen and brought us oranges, apples, yogurt and amazingly she also included muffins, sausage patties with English muffins, milk and orange juice. Seriouslyâ€¦this is SERVICE!!! The type of service you donâ€™t find everywhere. She went above and beyond our expectations and if I remembered correctly her name was Daphneey. The room was clean and nice, with everything you could possibly need. The bar was awesome with candles lighting up in every table. Shawn and Alex at the bar were amazing. The heated outdoor pool was relaxing. My wife and I were really impressed with the overall appearance of the hotel, the staff and especially the comfort of the beds. Thank you Hotel Le Marais, your immediate staff, the front desk staff, to the housekeeping departmentâ€”each person made my wife and I feel welcomed, cared about and treated like a friend. We will definitely comeback again and again!!!!!

reviews.author: Isai Johns
reviews.content: This hotel is newly renovated. It looks great today. They cut corners in renovation, for example, they didn't replace the elevator which is painfully slow. They used cheap doors and you can hear right through them. While waiting for an elevator we could clearly hear a shower in one room and a whoopie session in another. The complimentary breakfast is lame. A few boxes of cereal and bagels laid out in the bar, not a service person to be seen. That said, the bed was great, free wifi is appreciated,location is great and quiet. The rooms are pretty, small but nice.

reviews.author: Athena Feil
reviews.content: I recently had the pleasure of staying at the newest French Quarter property, Hotel Le Marais. While still undergoing the final touches of a massive renovation, this is a beautiful little gem right off of Bourbon Street. Upon arriving, parking can be a little tricky, as with all of the boutique hotels in the quarter. But valet was great. Lobby was small but very efficient, with the hotel lounge overlooking a stunningly beautiful courtyard, complete with a small pool. The rooms were very nice and stylish,. The beds were incredibly comfortable. LOVED the step in shower. Although no tub, the shower was great! And, even though you are less than a block off of Bourbon street, I couldn't hear any of the noise. This property is a real asset to the French Quarter and I would highly recommend it whether your going to the city for business or pleasure. Can't wait to stay again!!

reviews.author: Georgianna Nitzsche
reviews.content: I would highly recommend this hotel. The room was great, very modern, comfortable bed, and very nice and clean room and bathroom. The restaurant and bar are amazing. They have a few tables with beer taps at the table very cool. The bar is very modern and well stocked, if Abby is your bartender then your in for a treat, she is the best. Great staff and service. I stayed with my uncle and his friend booked the room and he did not know I was coming so when we asked for a room with two beds they had nothing available so they took some $$ of our bill and gave us two money vouchers towards the bar or restaurant. Great location a few blocks from bourbon street so you don't get all the noisy rift raft at night, and walking distance to Jackson square and the Frenchman. If Acme has a line 30 deep which it usually does go across the street to Felix's its better and is where the locals go, huge fresh oysters the best I have ever had. Enjoy the devils play ground I did.

reviews.author: Doyle Trantow
reviews.content: Recently stayed there for a night. It was my first trip to New Orleans, and the staff (especially Helbert) had great recommendations for food and entertainment. They were incredibly nice. Parking was cheapish and on-site, so I just drove right up to the hotel and valet-parked. The room was what you'd expect from a W: stylish, comfortable bed, nice bathroom. There is some construction going on at the hotel, which is sort of annoying, but it was not a problem on my floor. There's a great pool as well.

reviews.author: Princess Reichert
reviews.content: I had a pretty nice stay for three nights at the W under renovation in the French Quarter. Great location, nice staff, service variable, but OK, and I am fussy. Was in a new room. The good news is that unlike most W's there was enough light for my aging eyes. The bad, the new decor is really terrible. Why didnt they keep it simple instead of painting the walls orange with an eyesore of a design as the focal point of the room and the ugliest carpet I have ever seen. Could have been amazing, now certainly comfortable, and the good part is you close your eyes at night and dont have to look up at the scary design above you. I'd stay again, but sorry for the poor designer.

reviews.author: Maia Wehner
reviews.content: Love this place. The construction is a pain, but should be wrapped by summer. The staff was so over the top nice and helpful it made up for any jack hammers. Especially ask for Halbert and Daniel. The two guys were super cool and couldn't be more helpful. Southern hospitality at its finest. Ya'll.

reviews.author: Joannie Effertz MD
reviews.content: The staff at this hotel was the friendliest and most helpful of any hotel we've ever stayed in. Since there is currently construction going on at this W, it is difficult to give a fair rating. As SPG members we expected a better room. The only available room was small and there wasn't even a comfortable chair in it. But at least it was at the rear of the total away from the construction noise and jackhammers. A stay at this W will probably be better after the construction is completed and, hopefully, the rooms are upgraded a bit. The extremely friendly and accommodating staff and great location in the French Quarter are reason enough for us to return to the W on our next visit (after renovations are completed).

reviews.author: Arlie Leannon
reviews.content: We just stayed at the W New Orleans French Quarter. We had a very unfortunate experience with our room being located close to construction taking place in the hotel. The staff at the hotel was extremely attentive and did their very best to make things right for us. I can't say enough about the concierge Halbert. Truly a stand-out in service. Recommend verifying up front if construction is still taking place and if so doing what you can to ensure you are not going to be affected by it. Don't expect a fancy W - this hotel is older and going through remodel - but do expect excellent service. Some of the nicest people we met in NOLA - a place where everyone is friendly and nice.

title: Tamworth (England)

description: A hostel run by the Gatliff Hebridean Hostels Trust in the remote village of Rhenigidale, which is 6 miles from Tarbert on foot, or 13 miles by road. The road has only been there since 1990 and the only way in used to be on foot or by boat. The hostel does not take bookings, and has no phone.
name: Rhenigidale Youth Hostel

reviews.author: Garett Balistreri
reviews.content: We stayed at the Hard Rock Hotel for two nights in February. The hotel was great. The rooms were nicely decorated and the beds were quite comfortable. There was a stocked refrigerator in the room. However, the prices of everything was outrageous. Needless to say, we didn't touch anything in the refrigerator. A bottle of water was $7.00 plus a 20% surcharge and 6.5% tax. The pool was beautiful, it had a nice waterslide. The water wasn't very deep. The deepest part of the pool was 4ft 6in. We stayed at the hotel for the fass passes and early access to Harry Potter. It was definitely worth it. Harry Potter reached maximum capacity by noon and with the fast passes we never had to wait to get on the rides. The location can't be beat if your going to the parks.

reviews.author: Ebony Balistreri
reviews.content: Before booking our trip to Universal Orlando, I read a lot of reviews on TripAdvisor - thanks to those who take the time to post. Some were favorable, others not. With two teenage boys in tow, we opted for the Hard Rock Hotel. Our room was ready when we checked in (around 2pm) - clean and all in order. No problems with housekeeping during our stay. No problems with noise (we had a "garden view" room not facing the pool). Ate at The Kitchen for breakfast each day. A bit pricey but typical for resort restaurants. Food and service were good. Very easy walk to Citywalk and the two parks. Using your room key as Express Pass is the BEST benefit. We did not do the early AM entry to Harry Potter. Too many people do that so the lines wind up being long. During the regular hours we never waiting more than 30 mins. Priority seating at restaurants never was useful. Being able to charge to your room key is handy - especially when teens are on their own and need to purchase food. But watch out for limits as your cards get cut off if you exceed them.
